I Calld at mr Hamlins, they are well. mr Ballard at Doct Coneys & mr Pollards. Cyrus bot m 3 pints Spirrits, Cos t 1/20_. a t mr Hodges & others, a t Doc t Colmans.
very rainy. I was at mr Baxters till near night. mr Baxter gave me 6/, _lb Sugar. I Came home; his wife Seems more Comfortable. a t mr Baxters.

Clear & hott. Jon a here, killd a Lamb & Carrid his Son home. I was Calld to See mrs Pitts who appears to be Drawing near the Close of life. Shee Seeme resind in a great Degree to leav this world. Daugt Ballard & I watcht with mrs Pitts. a t Shubl Pitts s , his wifevery low.
Clear. I have Scolt my pickles. Ephm workt for Jon a , Dolly went to mr Burtuns. I have had 2 Gal ns Vinegar of him. a t home.

Cloudy & moderate. mr Ballard been up to for t , at y e hook. Cyrus to winthrop for Jon a s hors. I have helpt Do hous work & knitt. Pally Easty warp t & Beemd a web & went to Jon a s for hernes. I was Calld a t 10h Evn to See mrs Robbins who is unwell. I went to bed after 2h morn. a t mr Robbins.

X. Birth Jason Pierces Dag t . XX. Clear & pleast. mrs Pierce was Deld a t 10h morn of a Dagt, her first Child. I left her Comfortable & returnd to Col o Sewalls. I receivd 6/ by my D t Dolly June 6th of mr Pierce. a t Pierces & Col o Sewalls.Birth 14th. her first Born.
Clear foren, Cloudy aftr n . mr Ballard prepareing for his Tour. Selv Moor, mrs Blak, Holman, Swithams & Betsy Voce here. I have been makeing NapSacks. a t home.

Clear. mrs Pitts rose about an hour by Sun in y e morn, went out & milkt y e last milk from y e Cow into her mouth & Swallowd it. it is recommended as very Beneficial by mr Amos Page. her pain Still Continues & God only knows what will be the event. may Shee be prepared. I kep t my bed y e mos t of the Day. Dolly wen t to her Bro rs , Calld to See mrs Pitts, finds her very low. mrs Densmore here. a t mr Pitts s .

Clear. mr Ballard Sett out for Farmingt n . Dolly went to Jon as . I was Calld to mr Hamlins to See his Babe, it has a Disentary; his wife is mending. Calld to See Dagt Pollard, Shee is better. mrs Pitts is more Comfortable this Day. Sally wove 3_ yds, y e Boys finish t pulling our flax. at mr Hamlins, Pitts s & mr Pollards & Sons. Hannah 25 y r old y s Day.

Clear & Cool. I gatherd my Safron and Did other matters about house fore n . was Calld at 12 O Clok to See mrs Pitts who had an alteration. Shee Seemd in a Drousy State, her hands & feet Cold, but Shee revivd. I tarried & watcht with her, the Indiens were noisy which Disturbd her the Evn g but Shee had the most natural rest after 12 which I have known her in her illness. a t mr Pitts s & Hamlins. Indiens about this Evn, very Noisy.

Clear & warm. I went from mr Pitts s after Break fast, was at mr Craggs, Thwings, Kimbals, Hamlins, Pollards, Blaks & Calld again at mr Pitts s . Shee is a little more Comfortable than Shee was yesterday. Dolly & Sally went to mr Densmores. Sally went to Mr Grealys for a Bed Pann for mrs Pitts. mrs Hamlin made me a present of 6/ as Com . I bot 11 lb Veal, Cos t 2/6. a t mr Pitts s & other Neighbr, had 1-2/1 lb Butter of mrs Pollard. Shee Sent Dolly 6/.
Clear. mr Ballard Laying out house Lotts for mr Wesson. I houghd my mellons fore n , was unwell aftern. mr Isaac Savages wife here. Dolly Carded Cottne. at home. mrs Savage here.

Clear. I have wrote y s morn to my Bror Elijah Moore of Douglas. Silva Moore took Breakfast here & Sett out home. mr Ballard been to Es qr Conys & mr Burtuns. mr Green of Sandy river Sleeps here. Jon a Nichols here, receivd Letters to Carry to Oxford for me. James Sturges Came here yesterday. a t home. I wrote to Bro r Elijah Moore.
Clear. I have Done my house work. mrs Livermore here to See mrs Pitts. I have been at home. mrs Pitts is more Comfortable. a t home.

Clear. we removd mrs Hamlin ou t of the Chamber, Shee Seems more Comfortable. I went to See mrs Pitts after n , his mother there. I was at mr Wessons & Doct Colmans. returnd home, find my Dagt Pollard, mrs Barton and Polly Pollard here. a t Ditoes & others.

Clear & pleasan t . Dolly and Sally wen t this aftern to help mrs Hinkly quillt. Ephm workt for mr Desnmore. Selv Moore & James Sturges returnd home from Surveying. I have got in part of our Beets. mr Ballard arivd at home at 9 h Evn in health, may we take a Seutalle notice of the goodness of provedince. at home. mr Ballard & his men returnd from their Tour of Surveying.

Cloudy. I have been a t home. Dolly & Sally quillting for Dagt Pollard. Waid, Robbins & Livr mr workt here. I paid Doct Cony the money Agust s Ballard left for him. a t home. y e Girls at Son Pollard.

Clear. we made three attempts to have Dolly Sett up & Shee allmost fainted. Shee Seemd a little revivd aflern when we helpt her onto our hors. Shee wrode to the river & was Conducted home by water. Shee was very much overcome by watking from y e water to y e house. I pu t onions to her feet, gave her Castor & Shee Seems Some more Comfortable. a t Detoes. we brot my Dag t Dolly home. I Calld to See mrs Pitts, find her a Little moreComfortable.

Clear. Cyrus wen t to See how mrs Pitts is, finds her as well as Shee was yesterday. mr Ballard been fixing for his Tour & planking the Seller. I was Calld to Shub l Pitts s (watcht) his wife was very ill. mrs Abbott Setts with me. a t mr Pitts s . Dolly Sleepsat Son Pollards. mrs Porter at Pitts s .
rain. Augustus Ballard here. I have been at home. a t home.
Clear. Sister Williams Dind here. Dolly Came home, Sally Cocks returnd at noone. I have been at home. a t home. Sister Williams here.

X. Birth Nabby Towers Son by Moses Parmer. XX. Clear, Serene Day. I was Calld to 4h morn to See Nabby Towers who was in Labour. Shee was Deld at 11 h 30 m of a Son which Shee DeClard that Moses Parmer was the father of. I tarried & Dind. Esq r Haywood there. I arivd at home at 3 h pm, am fatagud. Dolly is unwell, has a Blister which lames her very much. at mr Lathrop. Birth 40th. receivd my fee Oct. 2nd by mr Daw a t the hook.

E X. Birth Wil m Whites. 4 th Son. XX. Clear & very pleast. I was Calld belween 12 & 1 h to See William Whites wife who was in Labour. I Crost y e river & went Down on hors back. my Patient not very ill. I laid down & took a fine nap. Shee Calld on me about Day Break & was Safe Delivd a t 6 h 20 m of a fine Son which waid 11 lb. I left her & infant Cleverly & arivd at home at noon. Sally Cocks & Patty Levermore ferried me over. mrs Blanchard, Brown, Winstow, Page & Adkins attended. mr Ballard & Sons & mr Perhan are gone to meeting. a t Wil m Whites. Birth 41s t . I receivd 6/as a reward.

E Snowd. I left mrs Bracket as well as Could be Expected. Calld & found mrs Burtun Cleverly. a t mrs Baxters, Shee is much the Same, at Son Pollards & Jonas, all well. little Jon a Came here at Evn g Conducted by James m c Clewer. at Bracketts & others.

E rainy morn. mr Ballard & Sons wen t to meeting. I attended aftern. th e Ordinance of Baptism was administred to mrs Pitts after Divine Service was over. Shee is as well as Shee has been for Some Days. I went to See mr Hamtins Child, find it very low. Dolly is a little Easier. a t meeting & at mr Hamlins & Pitts s . mrs Pitts Baptised y s Day.

E Clear & Cool & windy. my famely all went to meeting fore n Except my Self. I went after n . Calld at Son Pollards, and Drank tea. I was Calld after I Came home to See Saml Norcros s wife who has the rash. at [meeting] & at Saml Norcros s .

E X. Birth mr Kimbals Son. XX. Clear par t of the Day. I Slep t this morn, was Calld a t 9 h morn to See mrs Kimball. Shee was Safe Delivd of a Son at 4 h am. I receivd 6/ as a reward for my Service & returnd to mr Pitts. mrs Porter & Richd Foster Watcht. mrs Pitts rested Better y s night. a t mr Pitts s & Kimbals. Birth 32 nt .

E Clear. A mr Smith from Worcester here, he wishes to take a hors to Sandy river for mr Ballard to Come home on, he has mr Kenedys is on his way. Dolly is not So well as Shee has been of late. Sister Will ms , mr Pitts, mrs Barton, Abbot, his wife, Drank Tea here. Harry Smith & Liverm r , Sally Williams, mrs Densmore, S. Damrin & Sam Parker were here. at home. Sister Williams & others here.
Clear & Cool. I gatherd Beet Seed. mrs Partradg here. I have been ["at home."- implied] a t home.
Cloudy. Sister Wil ms went from here home wd bound. I have knitt part of a Stockin foot for Dolly & Some on mr Ballards. it has raind aftern. a t home. Sist Williams went home.
Clear. I have been at home, Dolly to Son Pollards. Son Town here, his famely well. at home.

Clear part of y e Day. Dolly put new Triming on my Case Draws. we movd it into her Bedroome. I have not Done much work, have not got over my fatague. Sally washt. they Dres t 9 Chickens for Cooking. at home.
Snowd & raind. I have been at home, hear that Son Jon a is Sick with the mumps. at home. widdow Fortis had a Son born.
Clear. I Did my work & went to See Dagt Pollard, find her a little more Comfortable. mr Ballard & Cyrus at Coart. a t Son Pollards. Sally Cocks returnd.

Clear & warm. I tarried with mrs Fillebrown till 3 h PM when her Marm Came, & I Came home. left my Patent as well as Could be Expected. receivd 12/7 as a reward. the Cap t is Confind by Sickness, but we hope he is on the recovery. I left mrs Wesson, Duttun, Conry, & mrs Lois Harris with them. I wrode home with Nathan Wesson. a t Ditoes Capt Fillebns, receivd 12/7.
Clear. I have been a t home. mr Ballard to a Church meeting. mr Densmore had a quilting & hushing. my young folks there, Came home late. a t home. my Girls at mr Densmores.
Clear & Pleast. I went to See mr Parkers Child n , find y m better. I was Calld to mr s Baxters, walk t part of y e way, wrode part. the Patient rested very well. a t Ditoes & mr Parkers.
A very rainy Day. I have been ["at home." - implied] at home.
Snowd, the ground Coverd foren, mostly Gone before night. I have been working on the raggs, Dolly helpt me. She wrode to mr Burtuns, bro t a pitcher home which Shee bot of him, Cos t 9/. at home.
Clear & pleas t . mr Ballard makeing Plans. I have been at home. Leut H. Smith and wife & Polly Livermore Spent Evning here. I hear that Son Jon a is very unwell. a t homee. we had Company.

Clear & very warm. I have been with mrs Pitts till Sun Sitt. was then Calld to the wife of Rich d Foster who is in Labour. I had no Sleep this night. I Did not Expect to find mrs Pitts alive when I returnd. at Ditos & Rich d Fosters. mrs [Pitts] has Seen 22 years this [night].

Clear. mrs Black here, had a Small Tumbler full of my aloetic Tincture, paid me /9; Shee had 1 oz Safron allso. Cyrus is Gone to pittst n , Ephm to work for mr Pitts. Son & Dagt Pollard, Dag t Ballard, mrs Pierce & Barton took Tea with me. Cyrus wen t to See them home. a t home. my Childn & friends here.

X. Birth Chandler Robbins s Son. XX. Cloudy. mrs Robbins Linguerd till 4 h pm when her illness Came on. Doct Parker was Called but Shee Did not wish to See him when he Came & he returnd home. Shee was Safe Deld of a Son, her first Born, at 10h 30m Evn & is as well as Can be Expected. I tarried & watcht. mrs Ney was there and Shee only, Excep t y e famely. a t mr Robbins. Birth 42 nt .

Clear & Cold. the ice Stop t here this night. I have been at home. Dolly & Sally went to help mrs Hinkley quillt. Ep h Laiton Sleeps here. mr Ballard to y e fort. at home. my Girls to mr Hinkleys. river Closed y s night.

Cloudy. I went to See my Son Jon a , find him a little better; to See mrs Baxter, Shee Seems mending. old mr Pollard is not So well as Could be wisht, nor mrs Barton. at Son Pollards, his Dads, mr Pitts.

Clear. the Boys wen t to fince the Stacks of hay. Sally wen t to See mrs Pitts, finds her more unwell. I had been to work in my Gardin & was fatagud, but went & watcht. Shee has a Disentary and much grip. a t Shubal Pitts s , his wifevery Sick. I watcht. wrote to Doc t Barton.

Clear. my Dag t Pollard Came here foren, mrs Davis aftern. Shee went to Col o Norths. I was Calld in hast at 8h Evn to See mrs Pitts who had an altaration & thought near Expireing. I watcht, mrs Livermore allso. mrs Pitts revivd. at Shubal Pitts s mrs Livermore &I watcht. Dagt Pollard and mrs [Davis] here y s Day.

Clear. mr Baxter bro t his wife here. Shee wrode up to the mast Landing allso. mr Ballard been to mr Pollards & is gone to the hook. Mall w Haywood Esq r here, paid his taxes & took a receipt. Dolly been making Patch work. I began me a Stockin. a t home. mr Baxter & his wife were here.

X. Birth Alvin Neys Daug t . XX. Clear Excep t a Shower about noon. I was Calld by Alvin Ney to See his wife who was in Labour. Shee was Deliverd a t 10h morn of a Daugt, was Exercised with pain & fainting after delivery. I made use of Camphr & other remidies. Shee revivd & I left her & Child Cleverly at 2h pm, receivd 6/ as a reward. find y e South Side of our Seller Cavd in on my return. Dolly Carding & Spining Cottne. Shuball Pitts & wife here. a t mr Alvin Neys. Birth 30th. a SecondChild. mrs Pitts here. mr Ballard been to the Parrish Meeting.

Clear. I have Brewd & went to See mr Parkers youngest Child who is unwell, afflicted with Worms. mr Ballard & Perham wen t to Es qr Coneys to make a return of their Survey. a t mr Parkers. mr Ballard & Perhambeen to Esq r Coneys & returnd a plan.

Clear. Cap t Dean took Breakfast, Son Pollard Dind, my Dagt & mrs Barton went home. I have been at home. there were 4 men Came and Slept by our fire, their Boat grounded on the gravil bed. a t home. mr Henry Smith & mrs Sally Williams were Joind in Marriage yesterday.

Clear. mr Ballard was at Esq Coneys & the hook, he took up two notes at mr John Sheppards. Dolly and Sally went to help Dagt Ballard quilLt a coat. I have been at home, knitt on my Stockin. a t home. my Girls went to Son Jon as ,he is Some better.
Clear & warm. Sally Came home near night, her Brother is very Sick. mrs Pitts wrode our hors home & Dolly returnd from Son Pollards. they have quillted a Bed quillt Since Shee went up there.

Cloudy morn, raind before noon. mr Ballard finisht giting his meddow hay & returnd home at 2h PM. mrs Barton & Polly Pollard went from here near night. my Dagt Pollard tarried all night. Hannah Seemd Some unwell. a t home. mrs Barton went from here. we finish t haying.

Clear. mr Ballard has been to y e fort & hook, Cyrus to work up Streat helping remove mr Pitts Store. Selva & James wen t away after Dinner. Dolly went to mr Shepherds, Bot me 1 yd & 1/4 peirshon @ 7/6, 2 yd s ribbond @ /7 1 Skein Silk /4. I have been helping Do house work. Ephm was Sies d with a Severe fitt between 11 & 12 h Evn, it was on him near an hour. we Calld mr Livermore & his wife, they tarried till he Seemd to be likely to rest, and went home. at home. Ephm had fitts this night. mr Livermr & wife were Calld [here turn] before morn.

Clear. mr Ballard is gone to mr Follets. mr Densmore Butched a Swine for us. Dolly is mending Capps for me. mr Baxter & wife Calld here, Shee Seems more Comfortable. I finisht a Stockin and began an other. mr Ballard bot 6 lb Butter of mr Cumings. at home. mrs Baxter here.

E Clear & Pleast. we had Son Jon a s Sleigh. Dolly & I wrode to y e hook to meeting. I was Calld at 9h Evn by Asa Williams to See his wife. I gave her medesin and used means which gave relief, tarried all night. at hook meeting & at Asa Williamss.

E Clear & very pleast. mr Ballard, my Sons & Sally Cocks are gone to meeting at y e hook. Son Jon a here, brot his oldest Son. Eph went & Carried him home after meeting. Dolly returnd, is well as usual. Polly Livermore & 5 gentlemen here. I have been at home all Day. at home. Sally Came home. mr Pet r Cleark went from home this night, we fear he is lost.

Cloudy & Some rain. I left mrs Williams Comfortable as we Could Expect and Came home. Calld at mr Craigs, all well there; at Son Pollards, Doore. they and Son Jonas famely are Cleverly. mr Ballard gone up to fort, Cyrus to hook, Ephm to meddow. mrs Baxter been here to Day. at mr Williams & Crages.

Clear & pleast. mr Baxter and his wife were here. Shee Seems much as Shee was when I last Saw her. Dolly is gone to mr Pollards to work. I have been at work on my Stockins, finish one and began the other. Phil p Bullin Sup t here. Polly Livermore been here y s day. We are informd that mr Peter Cleark went away from home last Evn g & is not ye t heard of. he has been in a Sort Delareum for Some months pas t . a t home. Dolly went to mr Pollards.

E Clear. mr Ballard & I went to meeting all Day, Dolly & Sally aftern. Dagt Pollard wen t home, Dolly tarries there y s night. mrs Barton at mr Pitts s , Shee is much as Shee [was]. at meeting & at mr Hamtins & Pitts s .
at home. Clear & Cold. Dolly at Son Pollards & mr Blacks. at home.

Cloudy morn. Clear after 9 h m, a very high wind. I finisht footing a pair of Tow hoes of mr Ballards. Dolly made a hat for Jon a s wife & one for Patty Easty. Sally washt. at home. Dolly made 2 hatts.
Clear & pleast. I was a t mr Craigs, Burtuns & Thwings, all well. I bo t 1/2 lb Snuff 1/2. Sally & Dolly quillting for Sally Williams aftern. at mr Craigs & others.

Clear. I have been at home. we Baked Some Pumpkin & Apple pies. Cap t Stackpoole, his Dagt Dingley, her inft and made Sleep here on their way to the hook. Dingley going to hous keeping there. a t home. Cap t Stackp l Sleeps here.
Clear. I help t about house & pick t wool. Polly Pages marm Sleeps here. Golly has been to See her Sister, finds her a little Better. mr Ballard & Cyrus been at Coart. at home. mrs Page Sleeps here.

Clear foren, Cloudy & Some rain after n . I have been gathering Beens & radish Pods. Cyrus went to Jon a s , Ephm to Hauling timber for the Bridg over Joness Stream. a t home. Jason Pierce removd from my Sons.

X. Birth mr Felps s Son. XX. Clear. Ephm had a fitt at day break, has been very sick till after noone. I was Calld at 3h pm to See mrs Felps who was Deld at 6 of a Son, her third Child. I left her & infant Cleverly & returnd home at 9, find Ephm more Comfortable. Cyrus been to Pitts t n to mill. a t mr Felps s . Birth 38 th .my Son Ephm had a fitt this morn.
rainy. I was a t mr Baxters all Day, went to bed, Slept very little. a t mr Baxters.

Cloudy. I Scolt my Pickles & went to See mrs Baxter who, I think, is better tho Shee is not of that mind. I went to See mrs Partradg, find Shee had an ill turn and is better. I was a t Son Pollards, their babe has one tooth. at mr Baxters & others.

Clear. mr Ballard Sett ou t to Survey Land beyend 25 mile Pond. I have been futed a pair hoes for Ephm. Cyrus brot 2 Dishes, 12 plates, one Ch r Pott which Dolly bot of mr Burtun. I have been at home. a t home.

Clear. I have been Cleaning my Mustard, Galhering herbs and other matters. Dolly wint to mr Pitts s , Sally to Colo Norths, Cyrus to mr Burtuns for Vinagar; got 1 Gal n . he is 38 y s old y s Day. a t home. Cyrus is 38 years old y s Day.

E Clear. Ephm was taken with an Other fitt about 2h this morn. we Calld mr Livermore again, thinking he was Expireing. he Came to his Sinces in Some Degree & Slep t quietly before Day. mr Livermore tarried till morn. I laid down on ye bed with my Child & Slept a little. we were So fatagud we [did] not attend worship. a t home. mr Ballard at meeting. Son & Dagt Pollard [here]. Ephm is Better.
Cloudy. mr Robbins went and brot mrs Voce to Nurs his wife, & Conducted me home in a Boat. he gave me 18/ as a reward. I found mrs Baker at our house on my return. a t Ditoes. receivd 18/.

Cloudy morn, Cleard of at noone. Dolly wen t to mr Pollards to work, Shee Seems to be more unwell than Shee has lately been. I have been at home. mrs Smith Calld here, Son Jona here at Evng. a t home. Dolly is gone to mr Pollards.

Clear & remarkable pleast for Christmas. I went to Son Pollards, little Jon a Ballard is unwell, the res t of their famelies are well. Dolly & Sally have washt, Scourd my puter & washt the Kitchen. I am informd that the wife of Richard Foster is very Sick of a feavour. at Son Pollards. Jon a Ballard is unwell. I began a mitt n for Cyrus.
Clear & very pleasant. I have been at home, began a glove for mr Ballard. my Girls went to See Daniel Livermore. Selvst r Moore here. a t home.

Clear. I have been with mrs Pitts all Day, Shee has been very Sick. her husband returnd home, brot her Some Port wine. I left her at Sun Sett. Calld at Son Pollards, took Tea. Dolly is there. a t Ditoes. mrs Pitts very ill.

Clear & Pleasant. I went to mr Sheppards, bot 8 yds Baise which Cos t 17/3, four yd s black ribbin 1/4. I was a t mrs Coutches, M & D Sewalls, Capt Fillebrowns, Lathrops & mr Robbins s . mr Perham Came here & Sleeps. a t the hook. receivd 7/ of mrs Felps. Henrey mc Causland murderd mrs Warrin.

Clear foren, Cloudy aft n . I Sett out to go and See mrs Baxter, but found the wrideing So bad I returnd after going a little beyond our field. Son Jona Came & waited on his wife home. Cyrus went to take our hors home. Jack is here yet. Dolly is makeing a Cloak for mehitable Pierce. mr Ballard is makeing a Plan. a t home. Jona Came for his wife.
rainy. I tarried with mrs Cocks, Shee & infan t are Cleverly. rain & thunder ys night. a t [Charls] Cocks s .

E Clear Some part of y e Day. I have been a t home. mr Ballard & Cyrus been to meeting of y e fort, mr Perham to y e hook. Dolly not So well as to go to meeting. Sally went to her Dads yesterday and returnd y s Evn g . we are informd that Henrey m c Causland Cutt mrs Warrins throat yesterday. at home. we were informd of y e murder of mrs Warrin.

E Cloudy. I Came from mr Cocks by water, left all well as Could be Expected. I Saw a Schooner go up by mr Goodins and a boat allso. I find a pair of new Shoes & put them on, they fitt me very well. Selvst r G. Moore Sleep here. we were alarmd by a noise in the night, the Girls & I rose. a t Ditoes. left thm Cleverly.

E Clear. mr Ballard, my Self and Sons went to meting. I larried with mrs Baxter all night, Shee is more Comfortable. I went to See mrs Partrages, Shee has Soar nipples. at meeting & at mr Baxters & Partrags.

Cloudy. mr Perham left here homeward bound. Pally Easty here Spooling a web for Jon a s wife. I went to See mr Parkers Child, I think it is Some better. Selv a & EbnZ moore here this Day. mr Ballard been up to the fort. Snowd at Evn, a heavy rain with high wind y s [night]. a t mr Parkers. mr Perham wen t from here. Patty Easty here.

Clear. I went to mr Childs, Burtuns. he gave me 6/7 for assisting his wife in Labour. I Calld at old mr Pollards, mrs Baxter & Dagt Pollard Dind there. Roseanna Green was Sick with a pain in her head. I put her to Bed & Swet her. mrs Baxter had a turn of hysterics. a t mr Baxters & Son Pollards Burtins & old mr Pollards.

Clear & very pleas t . mr Ballard & S. Moore Left here y s morn Bound up river to run a line for Gov r Williamson. I have been at home. Sally went to the hook for Dolly a pair Shoes & other things. a t home. Polly Livrmr here at Evn. mr Ballard gone up y e river.
Clear. Sally Hamlin here to have a Coat made. Shee Sleeps here. I have knitt on my Stockin. Sally washt, her Bror Gersham here. I have been at home. a t home. Sally Hamlin here.
Cloudy, raind a t night. mr Waid here, Butcherd a Beef hei fer for us. I Cleand the feet & Tripe. Sally washt, Cyrus went to Pittsto n . a t home.

Clear and very pleast indead. mr Ballard went to Cap t Cocks s to have a Glass put in his Cumpass. he allso Sett out to Survey the Town of Varsalborough this aftern, Expects to be gone the remainder of thee weak. Dolly is gone to Son Jonas. I have finish t kniting a pair of mitts, the fifth pair Since this year Comminst. at home.
A S E wind & rain. we had a Swine Butchered by Silv a Moore and Tilton. mr Ballard went to the fort. I have been at home. at home.

X. Birth Ben n Preskots Dagt. XX. Clear. I was Calld a t 4 h morn to See Ben n Preskots wife. Shee was Deld at 6 of a Dag t , I lef t her Comfortable a t noon; receivd 7/6 as a reward. mrs Barton, mr & mrs Cocks here. Sally wen t home. I wrode my hors. Calld at mr Robbins s , Parmers, & Cap t F Browns & Saml Norcros s ; find Some well, others geting Better. a t Ben n [Preskots]. Birth37 th .
Clear. I pulld up Been & mellon Vines. we had a vere hard frost. I was Calld to See Dorcas Hodges who is very ill with feavour and Cankar. old mrs Pollard there. a t mr Hodges to See a Sick Child.

Clear. Selva moore here. I have wrote to Doc t Barton, Bro r Collens and Sister Walers, & went to See mrs Parker who is very unwell. Dolly gone to mr [Smiths]. a t mr Parkers. wrote my friends at Oxford. Silva moore Sleeps [here].

Clear. Son Town took Breakfast here. the Company which Slept here went away. I am Calld on to go & See mrs Baxter, but had no hors & Could not go. Son Jona brot his wife and youngest Child here. mr Ballar d returnd at Evn, in health for which favr we Desire to be thankfull. a t hom. Cap t Stackp l went f m here. mr Ballard returnd.
A Severe Snow Storm. I was Calld at 6h Evn to go & See Cap t Sam l Huseys wife, went to mr Sheppards where they left me all night. at mr Sheppards all night.

Clear and pleas t . I went to See mrs Sheppard, find her as well as Could be Expected, her infant allso. they made me a pres t of 24 and a bottle of wine for Dolly. mr Ballard Surveying the river. a t mr Sheppards. Dolly Came home Sick. Lafaett is 2 years old.
Clear. I have been gathering Seeds. David Learned & wife & Child have brot me Letters from Sisters Barton & waters, of the 6 th & 7 th ins t . I had 1 galn Vinagr of Burtun.
rainy. mr Williams Dind here. I was Calld to See David Sewalls wife who is unwell. I was there all night. a t David Sewalls.
Cloudy. mr Baxter left home Bound to Boston, his wife took rheubarb. I was up with her till near Day, when Calld to See mrs Partradg who is in Lab r . a t Ditoes.
Clear. I was Calld at Evn to See a Child of ma gr Stickneys who is very ill. I tarried all night. I have been to mr Parkers, their Child Seems better. a t mgr Stickneys & mr Parkers.
Clear. I have been at home. Eph workt for Son Jona, Cyrus wen t to mill, had Jonas hors. a t home.

Cloudy. I arose this morn, found the ice was gone by our hous and that y e freshett higher than I Ever have Seene it. I Sen t Cyrus to See wha t Circumstance Jon a and famely were in, he returnd with news that his house was Covered Several feet from the bottom with water and that his wife and Child n were Carried in at mr Burtuns. I went Directly there, find my Dagt Comfortable to admiration. I tarrid with her all night, do not find as she has taken Cold, notwithStanding Shee was ou t three or four hours on her bed in a Sleigh. they did but Escape before their house was Startd from the foundation and the Chimney throun Down. they have Since found more of their affects than Could have been Expected. a t my Son Jon a s . his famely Drove out by thee freshet as ice being Jambd. his wife been Deld but 4 Days. the grait preserving mercy of God has wonderfully apeared for her and famely in their preservation.

Clear & very warm. I have been Doing house work & gathering herbs & Seeds. Cyrus Carrid Some radish Pods & beens to pickle to mrs Burtun, & brot 1 gallon of Vinegar. Son Town Sleeps here. a t home. Son Town Sleeps here.
Clear. mr Ballard very unwell with a Disentary, voids blood. I went to mr Bulluns, Baxters & Son Pollards. Snowd at Evn. a t mr Burtuns & others.

Clear till near night, it raind a little & Snowd at Evng. mrs Densmore here and warpt a web of 50 yds. her husband Came for, they Drank Tea here. mr Ballard has been fixing y e Barn So as to house our Cattle. a t home. rain & Snow.
Cloudy & Some rain. Ephm went to See how Jon a Balld Did, finds him more unwell. I have been at home. a t home.

X. Birth Joshua Learned Dagt. XX. Clear & very pleasant. I was Calld at 2 h morn to See the wife of Joshua Learned who was in Labour and was made the living mother of a fine Dagt at 10h Evng. Capt Neys Lady and mrs Dany assisted, tarryd all night. a t mr Learnds. Birth 1st for the pres t year.

Clear & fine weather. we removd Jona s wife and infant from mr Burtuns to Son Pollards. I tarried with her all night. Shee Seemd very much worried, Did not wrest So well as I Could wish. a t Ditoes. we removd my Sons wife to Son Pollards.

X. Birth Thos Stickneys Son. XX. Cloudy, Snowd Some. I was Calld to See the wife of Tho s Stickney who was Dild a t 5h Evn of her Second Son, a fine Child. I left my Patients Cleverly and returnd home at 8h. find Dagt Ballard & Childn are gone home. I receivd 6/ as a reward. a t Thos Stickneys. Birth 4.

Clear and moderate. I left my Child n as well as Could posably be Expected. Dagt Ballard Seemd more Composed in her head, may infinite goodness Grant her a Safe recovery, and may they and all Concernd give praise to his great name to him who is kind to the Evle and unthankfull, whose tender mercies are over all his work. I find Dagt Dolly more Comfortable, and the widdo Barton here to work with her. a t Son Pollards. I find my Dag t Dolly much more Comfortable.

D Cloudy. my famely, Except Sally & my Self, attended Devine Service foren, my Self & Shee after n . Tilton went from here to mr Livermores afternon. I Calld to See Son Jon a s wife, find her Comfortable even to admiration. may the benevalent authur of all our mercies Still Continue his Goodness to her and her famely. mr Gill and White here at Evng. at meeting, and at Son Pollards. Augustus Ballard and S. G. More Sleep here this night.
D A Severe Snow Storm. I was at home. mr Ballard went to meeting. a t home. R. Foster here.
Stormd part of y e Day. I was Calld to Cap t Husseys. it

Cloudy part of the Day. mr Ballard, Silva Moore & Eph m been Surveying the river. Dolly wrode to Son Pollards, finds Jon as wife as well as Can be Expected. mrs Barton & Sally Cocks washt, I assisted to Do kitchen work. at home. Dolly made a Gown for mrs s Abbott.
Cloudy. I was at mr Baxters till aftern. Calld at Son Pollards, Son Jona there has movd there with his famely. Sally Cox is gone to her Dads, returnd with Dolly, went to Livrm r s.

Clear & pleast for Decembr. I have finisht a Striped mitt for Ephm. Dolly & Sally went to a Daunce at mr Capins, were attended by a mr Lambart and White. I spent the Evn quite alone for Ephm was gone to y e hook & mr Morril up Streat. at home. my Girls to mr Capins.
Clear. I have been at home. Selva & Ephm Sett out to run the East line of this Town. at home.
Clear. Dolly went up to Son Pollards, mrs S. Hamlin house. I have finisht my Stockins. have been at home all day. at home. Sally H. & Dolly wrode up.
Cloudy part y e Day & very warm for Decem r . a mr Morrill Came here to make Shoes. I have pickt 10 o Z Cottne for mrs Densmore. a t home.
Clear. I have been at home. my Girls took a Sleigh ride. I began to foot a pair hoes for Cyrus. at home.

Cloudy morn, Clear middle of y e Day. Dolly & Ephm went to Dresden to See Edmond Fortis Executid for the Crime of Rape & Murder on Pameta Titlon of Varsalboro, on the 18 of May las t . Cyrus went to the Lodg. I was alone. Son Town Sleeps here. a t home. Dolly & Ephm went to See Edmd Fortis Executed.
rainy. mr Ballard & Cyrus went to meeting, our Childn, mr Pitts, mr Barton & Jo m c Causland Dind with us, the women & Childn Sleep [here]. a t home. a high wind [y s ] night.
Clear & Cold. Cap t Husey Came to mr Shep ds & Conducted me
Clear. mr Ballard went to Town meeting. I have been at home. I am much fatagud but knitt Some. at home.

Clear & Cold. Cyrus Conducted mrs Fanny Cocks home, Dolly wrode with her. mrs Learned here. mrs Barton Came here this morn. EbinZ Moore junr here. I have been at home, knitt on Ephms Stockin. he went to a meddow for hay for Jon a . Charls Cocks was here. at home. m Learned here. Fanny Cocks went home.

X. Birth David Sewalls Son. XX. Cloudy. at mr Sewalls, they were intimidated & Calld D r Page who gave my Patient [50] drops ot Laudenum which put her into Such a Stupor her pains (which were regular & promising) in a manner [Scant] till near night when Shee puk t & they returnd & Shee was Delivd at 7h Evn of a Son, her firs t Born. I left her Cleverly at 10 & walkt home. I receivd 12/ as a reward. a t Ditoes. Birth 39th. mr Perham Sleepshere. Dolly is unwell.

X. Birth mr Partridg s Dagt. XX. Cloudy. I was a t mr Partradg s with his wife, Deld of a Dagt a t 4h Pm. I tarried all night. She is Cleverly, y e inf t allso. mr Partradg bestowd 12/ as a reward. a t Ditoes. Birth 44th.
Cloudy. I have been a t mr Stickneys all day & night. his Child Seems Some more Comfortable, the phisick has opperated. a t mr Stickny s .

Clear. I went to See mrs Baxters, Shee thinks Shee is no better. John Chaml n Calld me from there to Anthony Brackets, his wife is unwell but Seems better, Slept Cleverly. I was there all night. Snowd at night. a t mr Baxters & Brackets.

X. Birth Ben n Fitches Son. XX. A foggy morn, Clear & warm afterwds. I was Calld to See Ben n Fitches wife at 1 h morn. Shee was Deld of a Son (her first Born) a t 5 h m. I left her Cleverly, her Babe allso, & arivd at home a t 10. receivd 6/ as a reward. Dolly is gone to Son Pollards. Sally finisht Bleaching y n , Cyrus went to Capt F Browns, bot 1 quart W E Rhum, paid Cash; had 2 lb Shugar, Dito of Butter & 1 lb Coffee which was not paid for. a t Ben n Fitche. Birth 35 th . at home.

X. Birth magr Stickny s 2nt Son. XX. Cloudy. I was Calld by Ma gr Stickney at 4h morn to See his Lady in Labour. Shee was Safe Deld at 2h pm of her 4th Child, a Son. her women tarried till near night when we left her & infant Cleverly. I receivd 6/ as reward. find mr Ballard is better & gone to y e hook. a t mgr Stickneys. Birth 43 d . mr [Ballard] is more [Comfortable. we ].

X. Birth mr Phinneys Dag t . XX. Snowd. mr Ballard finisht fixing the Barn to house his Cattle. I was Calld at Evn to See mrs Phinney, found her very ill. Shee was Deld of a Dagt, her first Child by her present husband, at 9 h Evn. I tarried all night, mrs Desnm r allso. my Palients Cleverly. I receivd 6/ as a [reward]. a t mr Phinneys. Birth 47th. his firstBorn, her first 6th Child.

Clear [&] very pleas t foren, Cloudy aflir n , raind at Evn. mr Ballard returnd from Surveying Gov r Williamsons Land in Fairfield. Dolly Spent aftern at mr Livermores. Polly Came home. a t home. mr Ballard returnd.

Clear & remarkable pleast. I left my patients Cleverly and returnd home at noone, receivd 7/6 as a reward. find my famely well. Dagt Pollard, mrs Barton and Polly Pollard were here yesterday. a t Ditoes. Dolly had Company yesterday.
D A Severe Storm, the wind S E. mrs Porter was ill, had women Calld. at Ditoes.
4] Clear part of y e Day. we finisht [ ] Dollys Bed quillt. I was at [home].
5] Clear. I was Calld at 11h last Evn to mr Gordins, his wife unwell. mrs Black Sent for me, I Could not go.

6] Clear and Cool. I was at mr Gordins, his wife was Deld (a t 4 h 30 m morn) of a Dagt. I left her & inft as well as Could be Expected and arivd at home at 11 h fore n . I receivd [3] lb Cottne @ 2/4 pr lb. I Dind and wen t to See mrs Black, find her better y n my fears. I Calld to See mrs Smith and mrs Capin and at Son Pollards. at Ditoes. Birth 14th. Calld to Thoms Kenneys a t 11h Evng.
E Clear & very pleas t . Dolly went to Son Pollards, Sally to her Dads. Juda Houston was here, Presented Mrss Learneds Compliments and her wish to See me. I have been at home all Day. at home.
7] Clear. I was at mr Kinneys, his wife very unwell all Day. Seems more Comfortable at Evn. par t of her women wen t home. a t Ditoes. my [patient] very ill.
D] Snowd. I was at mr Kinneys, his wife is better, her women all went home. I tarried this night. a t Ditoes.

E Clear & Pleast. I returnd. mr Ballard & Cyrus went to meeting at y e fort. Sally Cocks to y e hook. Ephm Seeking our Sheep. Dolly Came home at Evn g , informs me mrs Baxter is no Better, or Shee is insensable Shee is. at Ditoes. Dolly returnd. Ephm wen t home with Jack Ballard.
2] Clear and warm. I was at Ditoes. a t Ditoes.
3] Clear and warm. I was at Ditoes, am informd that John O. Pages wife was Deld this morn and her Child is Dead. a t Ditoes.
4] Clear morn, a very warm Day. I Came home from mr Kenneys. Crost on the ice a t y e hook. a t Ditoes.
Clear & Some Cooler, the ice makes in the river. mr Ballard is gone to Survey Land in Sidney. I have been at home, Sally returnd. at home. mr Ballard gone to Survey.

Birth Ben n Porters Son. Clear. mrs Porter in Labour all Day and was Deliverd of a Son a t 8 hours Evn. her Case was very Difucult and Shee was very fain t after Delivery till about midnight, when Shee revivd and got Some rest. mrs Carr watcht. at Porters. Birth 11th. A Ship [wreck] mentionedin this Days [herald].

5] Clear and very Pleast. I was Calld at 7h morn to mr Kinneys wife, Shee was Deld at 10h morn of a Son. I left her and inft Cleverly. Crost [part] by drawing ye boat on y e [ice] [thro the] water. a t Ditoes. Birth [15 th ].
was Dreadfull Crosing the river. I was Detaind there

Clear & pleast. I went to See Son Jon a s wife, find her tolerable Comfortable. I was at mr Capons, Blacks, Smiths, Pitts Store and Old mr Pollards. they were all well Excep t mr Stodard, he is mending. a t Son Pollards & others.
6] [ONE CORNER OF THIS ENTRY IS MISSING]. [ ] Cleard of a t Evn. I was [ ] 8h Ev to See mrs Lathrop at [ ]ok, found her So well as to Sleep in [ ] this night. I got Some Sleep mySelf. a t [ ] hook.
Clear and very pleasant. I finisht Eph ms Mitt, they are the first Striped ones I ever knitt. Polly & Alice Levermore here. at home.

D A Severe Snow Storm. Son Jon a here, his famely are all well. hee removd to the house at y e mouth of Jones Brook yeslerday. it is my Sincere wish that he may behave as becomes the head of a famely. a t home. the remains of Capt Daniel Savage were interd yesterday.
Clear & pleasant. I have been at home. mrs Barton went to mr Capins, Sally to School. a t home.

Clear and very Pleasant. I Came home from mr Boltons, left my patients Cleverly. Calld at Son Pollards, all well there. my Dagt and mrs Gill Came here. mr Ballard has been to Winthrop. at [Savage] [Boltons].

7] [Birth Lathrops Dagt]. Cloudy and Clear by turns. mrs Lat p was able to work till near SunSet when Shee was more unwell. Shee was Safe Deld of a Dagt a t 8h Evn and is Comfortable. this is her 5th Child, and one of them is Desceast . I was informd at Evn that a mr Watson was Drouned at Jones s mill this aftern. a t Ditoes. Birth 16th. mr Watson was Drownd at Jonsis. [5] D rainy morn, thunder with it. I left my Patients Cleverly and Came home at 10h morn, find my famely well. mrs Gill and all our men attended worship at y e hook. mr Ballard, my Self and Sons and S. G. Moore attended Funeral of a mr Watson who was Drownd yesterday. the prayer was made by y e revd mr Gillit. the remains were lodgd in the meeting house. John O. Pages infant was interd this day. a t Ditoes and at the funeral of mr Watson. P. & A. Livermores here at [Evn] [6]

Clear fore n . Sally Cocks went to See her Bror Gersham and Sally Huzey joind in wedlock. I have been at home. Dolly is not returnd. mr Ballard been to Col o Howards on business with mr Warren & S. Jewett. mr Kimbal & Hannah Cocks were allso married. I am informd. at home. Sally is gone to weding of her Bror and Sister, may they be prosper s .
Cloudy. I have been at home, Carded Cottne, Sally spun. a t home.

X. Birth mr Sheppards Dagt. XX. Clear. I was Calld to See mrs s Sheppard at 4h morn. Shee was Deld of a Dagt, her 4 th Child, at about 12 hours or mid day. I was Calld from there to Son Jon a s wife who is unwell. a t mr Sheppards & Son Jonas. Birth5 th . receivd 24/.
to his house in a Sleigh. I tarried there till the 13th

Cloudy foren. mr Ballard went to Survey for mrs Hussey. Cyrus, Silva & Ephm Surveying the road from mr Pollards to the Crotch by whites. Dolly to work for mrs s Burtun, Eph m & Sally to Singing School at Evn g , mr White Came home with them. Cyrus took the hors to Cap t Neys for his Dad y . I have been at home. a t home. mrs Barton has the tooth aick.
Clear and Cold, windy. I have been kniting. mr Ballard, Silva & Ephm Surveyd on the river below here and above to y e Mile Rock. mr Gill here at Evng. at home.
rainy. I left my Patients a t noon. Calld to Se mrs Baxter, find her better, but insinsible of the favour. find mr White puting triming on our Desk. a t Ditoes & mr Baxters.

Clear. I Came home from mgr Stickneys fore n. we roasted a Sholder of Lamb for Dinner. Dolly has made me a Double Gown, & a goun & Skirt this weak. mr Ballard is gone to the South Part y s town on E. Sid river to Demand taxes. a t Ditoes.

XX. Birth mr James Burtns Son at XX 11h 5m morn; mr XX Brackets Son a t 10h Ev n . XX. Snowd. mr Burtun Came to Bracketts for me about Day to go & See his wife who is in lab r . Shee was Deld at 11h 5 m fore n of a Son. I Dind there, was Calld back to mr Bracketts, his wife in Labour & was Deld at 10h Evn of a Son, her firs t born. Shee was very ill. I tarried all night. mr Bracket gave me 6/7 as a reward. mrs Bolt n Slep t there, the other women wen t home. a t mr Burtuns & Brackets. Births45 th and 46th. recd 6/7 of Bracket.

Snowd part of the Day. mr Ballard been out on business. Silva Moore went home. mrs Sally Church Came here to have a Cloak made, tarried y e night. I have been at home. mrs s Gill and my Girls Closd an oppiration for y e itch and washt, hoping they are Cured. at [home]. Sally [Cocks] Came [here].

E Clear. mr Ballard went to meeting at y e hook, Cyrus to y e fort. I went to See mr Robbins s infant, find it is better. Dag t Pollard here, Georg Brown & a mr Lovjoy. my Girls wen t to mr Densmores a t Evn. a t mr Robbins. Dagt Pollard here.

D Clear. mr Ballard attended Public worship at the hook. mr Bailey Calld here, took Tea and waited on mrs Church home. mr Capin and wife, mr Gill & Lambart & D. Livermr here at Evng. I have been at home. Polly Town here. a t home. mrs Church went home. Polly Town Came here.
E Clear. we wen t to meeting a t the hook, Sacramend administred there. Georg Brown Sleeps here. Jona & his wife here at Evng. a t [meeting] a t y e hook.
Clear fore n. mr Ballard went to Varsalboro. Bro r Town & Selva Moore Dind here. I have been Cuting & Sewing raggs for Coverlid s . Esqr Cony here foren. at home.

Cloudy foren, Clear aftern. Gersham Cocks here, all well at his Dads. I have been at home. I pickt 5 Chickins & finisht mr Ballards glovs. Dolly has Spun Six Skeins of hoes yarn, Sally Carded it. at home.

Clear. mr Ballard, Cyrus & Lilva at Town meeting. there was but little Done Excep t Contend (a Grea t evil). I have hatcheld 14 lb flax from the Swingle. mr Gill Came for his wife & my Girls to go to Son Pollards, and they all went, Polly allso. at home. annuall meeting in this Town.
Cloudy. our men all went to Town meeting. I have been kniting. [mrs Gill] Sleeps here. I have been [at home]. at home. [Rebecka Bisbee Desceast.] [7]

Clear till near night. I went to mr Capins, find her mending; to mr Abbots, his wife is Cleverly; to mr Hamlins, his wife is unwell; at mr Dickmans, receivd 9/ on Benn Branches acoun t for assisting his wife in Child Birth. I Calld at mr Smiths and took Tea, went and Slep t a t Son Pollards. I was in great Danger by a fall from the hors but Escap t unhurt thro Gods preserving mercy. a t Son Pollards and other Neigrs. a rainy night. preservd when in Danger by a fall from a hors.
Clear fore n, Cloudy after n . I went to See Danl Livirmore, he is very ill with the mumps. at mr Livermores to See his Son Daniel.

Clear and Pleast. mrs Porter is as Comfortable but weak. I left her at 11h morn. mr Chambers accompanied me home. I arivd a t 8h Evng, find my famely much better than I left them. Dolly has wrode out this Day. Son and Dagt Pollard and mr Gill, and his wife returnd from winslow las t Sater Day. all well there. a t Ditoes. returnd home. mr Ballard is Surveying at Pittston.

Cloudy, Snowd a little, raind at Evn. mr Ballard & Silva Sett ou t to Survey at Pitts to n & Bod n ham. Cyrus went to Pitts ton, mr Gill and mr While here y s night. I have been about makeing Soap. at home. Rebecka Bisbee interd. [8]
till the morn of ye 24th when I Came home. mr Ballard

X. Birth Saml Husseys 3 d Son. XX. Clear. I was Calld to Cap t Husseys, walkt to the Loading place from there, Crosst the mountains of ice. arivd all most fatagud to Death, found his wife Deld of a Son. I returnd home by fort Weston, wrode our hors from mr Pollards. Dagt Ballard [Cleverly]. a t Cap t Husseys. Birth 8 th to which I have been Calld.
Cloudy part of y e Day. I have been at home about makeing Soap, have no good luck. at home.

Clear. I have been at home. Silva and Eph m , Foster & Morrel returnd from Surveying. Phillip Bullin informd me that Son Towns famely are well. mr Lambart and White Spen t Evng here. a t home. Dolly wen t to mr Densmores.
A S Easter, Cleard off after n . Son Pollard Came and Conducted his wife home. I was Calld to See a mrss Gordin who resides at mr N. Shaws, tarried there all night. a t [mr] [Gordins].

Clear. I have been very unwell all Day, feel better at Evng. Ephm went to the hook mill, brot home 1 bushl wheat, 1_ of indien meel, one of which he purChast at the mill, paid 6/. at home unwell. Sally Hamlin here.

Clear & very Pleast. I have been Doing my house work. mr Ballard is gone to Survey for Asa Emerson. mr Bullin here, informs Hannah Cocks was maried yesterday. a t home. mr Ballard Surveying for Asa Emerson.

X. Birth Charls s Cocks s Son. XX. misty. I was Calld out of Bed by Charls Cocks to So his wife who was in Labour. I Crost on the ice at Martins. Shee was made the Living mother of a Son, her first Born, at 10 & 20 m morn. I tarried with her all night. the ice moovd by there. a t Charls Cocks s . Birth 48th. receivd 6/7 d .
A very rainy morn & heavy wind. a t Son Jonas.
Clear and very Cold, froze the ground all day. I have been kniting. mrs Gill and Sally washt. Dolly went to Son Pollards aftern, all well there. I have been at home all day. at home.

X Birth mr Claps Son. XX Clear. mr Hodges I was Calld to See mrs s Clap who was in Labour. I left home at 4h pm, walkt as far as mr Hodges, wrode with Edw d Savage Jun r the res t of y e way. my Patient was Deld a t 5 of a Son and is Cleverly. I tarried all night, Slep t Some. a t mr Claps s . Birth 21 st . I saw mrs s Jones there.
when I Came home, and returnd there again and tarried all

X. Birth David Pollards 2 nd Son. XX. Clear. I was at home till Evn, 9 O Clock, when I was Calld to See David Pollards wife who was Dild of a fine Son at 10. I tarried all night. mr Stodard is very Sick at that house, Dilarious. Doc t Page there to visit him, he was Calld in y e night. a t mr Pollards. Birth 9 th . David Pollards wife is 27 years old this day. receivd 6/7 Novr 20 of Old Lady Pollard.
Clear and very Pleasant. Sally washt house & Scourd puter, Dolly asisted Some. I have been at home. Son Jon a here. a t home.

X. Birth Ben n Branches Son. XX. Clear the most of the Day. mrs Branch was Deld of a Son at 9h 30m morn. I left Cleverly a t 11, Calld at Nath Runels and mr Dyers and Esqr Runels. returnd as far as Son Pollards and went to See mrs Capin, find her not So well as Could be wisht. was at mr Gills, they are well. mrs Abbot is unwell. I Calld to See mrs Parker who is very unwell. Dolly was at mr Pollards, Capins, Gills and mr Burtuns. my Girls have Made me 2 Barrils of Soap this weak. at [mr Branches]. Birth 18th. receivd 9/ at DeckmansStore April 27 th by Branches order.

5] [ONE CORNER OF THIS ENTRY IS MISSING] Cloudy part of y e Day & Cool. mr Wade, [John] Livrmr Jun r , & James Kenady went [ ] ploughing. I have workt at [ ] Soap again, have not Sucsceeded. at home. Ephm [ ].
D night. Came home again the 14th.

D Clear. I tarried with my Patient part of the Day & all night. Calld to See mrs Baxter, Shee is recovering. mrs Voce & I win t to mr Childs at Evng, all well there. mr Stodard a little more Composd. a t Dittoes.

D rainy. mr Ballard went to meeting to thee hook. I was not So well as I Could wish. Augustus Ballard Dind with us. we had a Loin of Veal roasted. mr Barnabas Lambart auskt our Consen t that he might Marrie our Daug t Dolly. at hom. we Consentd that mr Barn as Lambert Should marrie our Dag t Dolly.

D Clear & very pleasant. mr Charls Gill and mrs s Betsy Barton were united at this house, the Ceremony performd by Wil m Brooks Esqr. Son Jon a , his wife, Pollard and his, mr Lambart, Hews and Polly Town, and Salv G. Moore and all my famely were present. a t home. mr Charls Gill and mrs B. Barton were united. Dagt Dolly has the Mumps.
Clear morn, raind a t night. I was at the hook. I Saw mrs M c Causland there. find mrs Robbins Cleverly. a t the hook.
at Capt Huseys 7 Days.

Clear. I tarried with my patient till after Dinner. left her and infant Cleverly. mr Stodard Seemd to have made feavour. Doc t Page Bled him in y e feet this morn, he has been bled, Phissicked and Btistered before, in his Sickness. I Calld at mr Abbotts, Capins, and Son Pollards. Dagt Ballard is not so well as Could be wisht this aftern, her Sister Trask was there with a Lamb Son. a t Dittoes & other Neighbours.

X. Birth mr Childs s Dagt. XX. Clear & pleast. mr Gill & wife Sett out for Winslow. I was Calld at 11 m to See mrs Child. Shee was Deld at three pm of a Dagt. Ben n Porter Came there at Evn for me to go and See his wife. I find Dolly very ill with the mumps when I Came home. Jany 29 = I recd 2 Crown s . a t mr Childs. Birth 10th. Dolly is very ill. Ben n Porter Sleeps here. mr Ballard is goneto Sidney.

rainy. mr Ballard wen t to mr Jonses. I have been mending Shirts, apron & Trousers. mr Lambart & my Dagt Dollys intencions of marriage were made public by posting at y e meeting house. a t home. mr Lambart and Dolly Publisht.
Clear Day, Cloudy Evn. Dolly went to mr Childs to work this aftern. mr Ballard Saw Rich d Foster, his wife is better. at home.
Clear and pleasant. [ Daft]. I Sett out with mr Porter (left Dolly very Sick). we arivd at 2h pm, mrs s Carr and her Sister there. I was falagud. a t Ben n Porters.

Birth Savage [Boltons] Son. Clear and pleasant. Dolly is quillting a Coat for her Self. I Combd flax foren, was Calld to See mr Partrages Child who is very unwell, has a Cough. I was Calld from there to See Savage Boltons wife who was in Labour and was Deld at 6h pm of a Son. I tarried there all night, my Patient Cleverly. a t mr Partridges & Savage Boltons. Birth 12th. receid 1_ Bushl of apples in the fall, not very good.

rainy. mr Baxter Came to Son Pollards for me to go and Attend his wife in the operation of an Emetic. it had a favourable opperation and I Came to mr Blacks [&] took Tea with her, went to Son Pollards and Slept. a t mr [Baxters] and others. Dolly bo t a Barril Cyder in a vessel, Price [21/].

Clear. I have workt in my Gardin. Capt F Browns Company were Calld to Exercise this Day. Phillip Bullin and W m Parmer & mr Lambart, Anna & Fanny took Tea here, and the Girls Sett out for home at Dusk. I planted Beens & Cucumb rs . at home. mis s Cocks & Bullin wenthome.
Cloudy. I have been at home, finisht Cyrus s mitts. at home.
Clear & Pleas t . I was at I was at [sic] Ditoes. Shee is Cleverly, and her famely. a t Ditoes. Son Jona 32 years old y s day.

Clear morn, Snowd Some aftern. I went to the hook with mr Chambers, Sent Some Spirrit and Sugar to mrs Porter. I was a t mr Sheppards, Robins, Parmers, Daws, Shaws and Cap t F Browns. mrs Livermore here on a visit. a t the hook. mrs s Livermore here. mr Ballard returnd.
X Clear and warm. Dagt Ballard and Dolly went to the Stores. hook, Dolly brot Crockery and 3/4 lb Snuff. mr Ballard & Selva went to Town Meeting. I have Sewd raggs for Coverled. a t home.

X. Birth Grefins Son. XX. Clear and pleast. I was at the hous of Brestow Grifins. this wife was Safe Deld of a Son at 6 hours pm. I receivd 6/ as a reward, returnd home by water. a t Grifins. Birth 26th.

XX. Birth Ezra Smiths Dag t . XX. Cloudy part of y e Day. I was Calld at 7h morn by mr Ezra Smith to See his wife who was Deld at 9 h 30 m of a fine Dagt. I tarried with her the Day and night. Shee is as well as Could be Expected. Shee has got y e rash. at mr Ezra Smiths. Birth 27 th . CaptDancy Left y e fort.
there to run Lines. Son & Dagt Town Came here last Evn
Clear. I was at Ditoes, all well. mrs Chambers there. a t Ditoes.
Cloudy and Some rain. Sally Hamlin here, Came yesterday. I was Calld to See Edmond Getchels wife who is unwell, tarried all night. at mr Getchells.

Clear, wind to y e Northerd. mr Kilridg Conducted his wife to Capt Deans Vessel at y e hook Bound to the South Shore. I Came from mr Smiths after assisting to git his wife up and to Bed again. Calld at mr Gills. Saw Son Town, his famely are well as usual. I was at Esq Haywoods and Son Pollards. my Dagt was here helping Dolly about her Bed quillt. at mr Smiths. receivd 7/6 as a reward. Polly [sic].

Cloudy and a little Snow. this Day is the annevercyra of my Birth. I have Seen 60 years roall away and but few more must look to See. God grant I may for Death prepared bee!!! I have been at home, finisht a pair of hoes for mr Ballard. a t home.
Clear, at Does & at Chambers. a t Ditoes.

Clear and Pleasant. I was Calld from mr Gordins to See the wife of Capt James Springer who was in Labour, and her Case was Supernatural. I tarried there y s night. a t [Ditoes] & at [Capt Springers].mr [Day ] house [ ] & all [ ] in it.

Clear. I am more Comfortable. mr Isaac Savage Second s & wife here on a visit. mr John Abbot Calld me at 6h pm to See his wife who is unwell. I was there this night. Shee and I Slept Some. mr Town took Breakfast here. Dolly went to Son Pollards. mrs s Cashin here foren. a t mr Abbots. mr Savage and wife here, Son Town allso.
Clear. I was at home, workt Some in my Gardin &C. mr Ballard returnd from Coart. Ephm has been to Winthrop for one Doz n Chairs for Dolly. a t home.

Clear & very Pleast. Sally returnd from weding. mr Ballard is gone to Varsalboro to Survey Land belonging to Saml Brown of Bilraca. I have been at home. Dolly is not returnd. a t home. we made [Sausag]. it is fouryears [Since] Dolly [has] been out [of] health.
Clear. at my Sons. Son & Dagt Town Calld there on y r way home. a t Ditoes. finisht Cyrus s hoes futing.
Clear. at Ditoes. mrs Chambers there, and mr Moore. a Storm at night. mrs Porter unwell all night. at Ditoes.

Clear and cold. mr Gill Conducted his wife home from our house to houskeeping. I wish them prosperity. they left here between the hours of 12 and 1. mr Waid here, Butchered a Calf for us. he had a quarter wt 18-2/1. EbenZ Moore Junr Sleeps here. Eph m brot Jack Ballard here, he is in Jackoa t and Trousers. Sally Cocks is Sick with a Coald. a t home. mrs Gill Left here and went to houskeeping. Sally Cocks is unwell.

Clear. I went to See mrss Parker, Shee is not So well as I Could wish. I Calld to See mrs s Kimbal, Shee is unwell; at mr Crages and Dind; at mr Thwings, they are as well as usual; at mr Capins, Shee is mending; at mr Abbots, Shee is feeble; a t Son Pollards, they are well as usual. I wrode his hors home. a t Ditoes and other Neigh s . Sally gone to her Dads. Cyrus Came home.

Cloudy and Some rain foren. I workt in my Gardin aftern, planted Beets and Mellon Seeds. the wind blowd Cool. mr Ballard been to Varsalboro. Silva Came home from Pitts t n . Ebenz Moore Sleeps here. at home.

Clear. I have been tending Cloath y t ws whitining and quilling y n for Dolly to weav, & working in my Gardin & kniting. Capt Ney Sheard our Sheep. mr Ballard Bot Cloath for a Coat for him Self. a t home.

G Clear. mr Ballard, Cyrus & Ephm attended meeting at y e hook. Sally went to her Dads & Polly Easty to Son Pollards, finds Dagt Ballard unwell. Expects Shee has got the rash. Augustus Ballard & mr Hodgden Supt here. a t home.

E Clear and pleast. Ephm and EbenZ Moore wen t to meeting at y e hook. Son Jon a and Pollard and their wives Dind and took Tea with me. Jona and his little Son Jack walkt home. Dolly wrode & Carried his wife & infant home. Sally Cocks is unwell yet. mr Gillit Descoars t from John V C, 39 vers. a t home. my Childn here.
D Clear. mr Ballard, my Self, Selva, Sally & Ephm attended meeting at the hook. mrss Waid was lost in the woods. Ebenz Moore went home. Dolly has been unwell y s Day. at meeting. Dolly is unwell.
D Birth Son Jon a s Dagt. Cloudy, Snowd Some. Dagt Ballard was Delivd of a Dagt at 3 h pm. mrs Woodwd & I tarried all night. I went to Bed & Slept Some. at Jonas. Birth 6th.

Birth Black Hitties Son. Snowd. I was Calld from Jonas to See Black Hitty. Shee was Deld of a Son before I arivd, found her Exercisd with pain, administred Something for her relief. returnd home by way of mr Husseys, all as well there as usual. I went to See my Dagt Ballard after I Came home, find her & infant Cleverly. Dolly is gone to work at Old mr Pollards. Sally Cocks gave me a Snuff Box. at my Sons and Nicholas s . Birth 7 th . this man is a Portugues who was Brot here by mrs s Hussey from Nantucket.

X. Birth mr Capins Dagt. XX. Cloudy the most of the Day. I was Calld by mr Capin at 5h morn to See his wife who was in Labour, and was Safe Delivd of a Dagt at 1 pm, and I left her and infant Cleverly and returnd a t 3. I was Calld to Cap t Molloys. at mr Capins. Birth 17 th . Snowd at night.

Clear. mr Ballard and Silva finisht planing. I have workt in my Gardin, Sowd Carrotts, Parsnips, Onions, Radishes; planted Cucumbers & Vermillion Seed. mr Ballard is gone to y e fort. a t home, workt in my Gardin.

Clear & warm. mr Ballard Sett out to asis t in Laying out a road to new Sharon on Sandy river. Ephm went and wrode the hors home. I have been at home, tending Cloath to whiten and Doing Divers other matters. at home. mr Ballard Sett out to Lay out a road.

Clear. Dolly put in a Bed quillt all tho Shee has been very unwell. mr Capin, Gill, Dagts Pollard and Ballard and mrs s P. Pollard Came and assisted her. the Gent mn Came to Conduct them home. a t home. we were quillting on Dollys Bed quillt.
Asa Cumings Expird this morn.
Clear. mr Ballard and Silva Sett out to Survey East andover. I have been at home, mended Cloaths. a t home. mr Ballard gone to Survey.
D a rainy Day. Cyrus went down to pitts tn. I have been very unwell. Jon a Dind here. mr Lamb t Came this aftern. a t home, unwell.
Clear. I Came home, wrode a hors of Son Jon as , find mrs Young here helping Dolly Strike A harnis. Doc t Cony here for a plan. a t Son Pollards.
rainy. I have been at home, work t Some in my Gardin between Showers. mr Capin wrote here. a t home.

Clear and warm. I workt Some in my Gardin fore n . was Calld to See Cap t Molloys Lady aftern, find her Exercised with weakness, Gave her Some Directions. Shee gave me 1/6 and I returnd home at Evn. mr Ballard been to y e fort to Survey for Es q Coney. Ephm workt on the road with y e oxen. at Capt Molloys.

Clear and pleasant. I went to mr Craigs, Shee made me a present of half yd Cambric & Do of CLaoth. mrs Burtun & Thwing there. I Calld to See mrs Child, Shee and infant are as well as Can be expected; at mr Pollards, Davids wife is about again. I Calld at Son Pollards & Jon a s , all well. a t mr Craigs and other Neigrs. mr Ballard went to mr Joh n Jone s . Ephm gone for hay.
Clear. mr Ballard went to Colo Howards. Dagt Pollard here. I have workt a little in my gardin, Sett Car Current Bushes &C. a t home. my Dagts here.
Clear and Pleas t morn. I have planted Beens in y e Door y d . Sow d Pees round the flax. mr Gill & wife and mr Lambart took Tea here. a t home.

D Clear and Pleast, not So warm as Some days pas t . mr Ballard, Ephm & Sally wen t to meeting at the fort. Cyrus Came home and wen t aftern. I have not felt So well as I Could wish. August s Ballard tarries here. at home. mr Savage and wife Sleep here.
and Dolly returnd home from there. at Cap t Husseys 5 Days.
rainy. I was Calld from mr Gitchels to See Brian t Fletchers infant which is very Sick; returnd at Evn. a t Ditoes & mr Fletchers.

X. Birth Georg Boltons Son. XX. Clear and very warm. Charls Cocks s wife was here on a visit. I was Calld at Evn to go to Georg Boltons. I had a viry fataguing Journey, found the patient Deld of a Son. I tarried till morn. I wrode mr Ballards hors. at Georg Boltons. Birth 29 th . receivd8/5 June 27 th 1797.
Clear and very warm. I workt in my Gardin, the weads had all most got the mastery. Selvester G. Moore Sleeps here. at home.
Cloudy fore n , Clear aftern. mr Ballard returnd at noon. Brother Moore Dind here. Fanny Cocks Sleeps here. I have been Sewing Raggs for Coverl d . a t home. Fanny Cocks here.

X. Birth Capt James Springers Dagt. XX. Clear and pleast. mrs Springer was Deld at 7h morn of a Dagt. I left her and infant Cleverly and returnd to mr Gordins, find her about. from there I returnd home, find my famely all well. mr Ballard is gone from home. Sally Cocks is gone to her Dadys. Shee went yesterdy. at [Capt] [Springers] and [Gordins]. Birth 13[th].

X. Birth John Abbots Dag t . XX. Cloudy and Some rain. mrs Abbot was very unwell all day, her women Calld at 1h pm and Shee was Deld at 3 of a Dagt, her first born. I went in at mr Blacks, his wife and mrs s Smith Calld to See mrs s Abbot. I went to See mrs Capin, returnd and took Care of my patients all night. a t mr Abbots. Birth 19th. receivd 6/as a reward.
D Snowd. Silva and Ephm went to Pittst n to Church, Sally & her Sister to Son Pollards. I have been at home, have taught little Jo na to read, he learns very fas t . mr L bt Supt here. a t home.
D Cloudy. mr Gill here last night and this Day & night. Silva & Ephm went to meeting at y e hook. Jessy Bullin Conducted Sally Cocks home. mr Lambert here at Evng. a t [home].

D X. Birth Capt John Moloy s Dagt. XX. rainy. I was Calld by Cap t Molloy about Day, find his wife very unwell. Shee had Some women Calld a t 10h M , and Some Came in at intermition. Shee was Deld at 1 h pm of a Dagt, her Second Child, both dagts. I tarried till near night when I left mother and Child Cleverly, and returnd. find my famely well. mr Densmores and Livrmrs Dagts here at Evng. mrss Moloy was unwell all Las t night alltho Shee Did not Call me till morn. a t Ditoes and Capt Molloys. Birth 20th. receivd 18/ as A reward.

Clear. I have been at home knitt , began to knitt a Stockin for Ephm. Sally & her Sister went to Singing at Evn. Richd Foster Supt here, he brot us a Bush l wheat. a t home. Dolly is a t SonJon a s to work.
Cloudy. Town meeting held at the hook, our men all went. I have been fiting raggs for Coverled. Silva Moore and Sally Cocks gone to a Daunce at y e hook. a t home. the [ ] to [Town] [meeting].

Clear and very windy. mr Waid workt here. mr Ballard and his men returnd from East Andivour, they are very much fatagud. I have been very unwell with a Could. a t home. mr Ballard returnd. Son Jon a s house was Burnt at Evining.

Clear. mr Stone & Child Calld here . mr Ballard Sett out for Pownalboro Coart, he is on y e Grand Jury. Dagt Pollard here with her Dear little Dagt. Shee, with Sally and Patty, went to mr Pages for Strauberies, mrs s Hodges allso. mr Town and Pollard here, Conducted them home. at home. Dagt Pollard here. mr Ballard to Coart.

D Clear part of y e Day, raind at night. my famely all at meeting, mr Bradford Performd. Cyrus Came here. mr Hamlin and wife, mr Pitts here. Son & Dagt Lambart here after meeting, mrs Rockwood allso. a t meeting.
Clear. I was at Capt Molloys. at Capt Molloys.

X. Birth James Blacks Daug t . XX. Clear foren. I went to the hook, bot a Brass kettle wt 5 lb a t 4/ pr lb of Capt FilleBrown. Cloudy aftern. I went to mr Pollards, was at mr Smiths, Pitts s , Gills and Son Pollards. Came home at 5, Sett Down to write to Doct Barton and was Calld off by mr Hodges to See mrs Black. Shee was Deld at 7 of a Daug t , her 4 th Child; number of Sex Equal. I tarried all night, my Patient was Exercisd with Some pain. a t y e hook & fort, atmr Blacks. Birth 22 nt . raind at night. fee receivdJune 4th.

Clear. I have been tending the Cloath and weading Beens & Cucumbers and other things. mr Gill here, his wife is unwell. Eph m went to winslow. Capt Nichols Dind here. at home. Capt Nichols here. Ephm went to Winslow.
Clear. I have been a t home, quilltd on Dollys Bed quillt. mrs Savage Came here aftern, helpt us and we got it out. mrs PeterSon here. a t home.

XX. Birth mr X Cains Dagt. XX. Augs t 22, 1799 recd 3/ by mrs Dingly. Clear. Sally Webber Came here, wisht to tarrie till the vessel is readdy to Saill. I was Calld at noone to See mrs s Danys infan t Son who is Exercisd with a relax. I dealt out a Small portion of Rhubarb for it. was Calld from there by mag r Stickney to go to mr Cains, find his wife very ill. Shee was Soon Deld of a Dagt. I had a fall from my hors on my return which hurt me. I found, when I arivd at home, that mr PeterSon has Sent for me. I Calld the Girls up and went to mr Densmores, but Could not hear from her. I did not know where to find her and relurnd home late. at mr Denys & Cains. Birth 32 nt . novr 26, 1796 receivd 6/ in part of fee by mrs Stickney. at mr Densmores at Evng. mrs Pelerson Deld of a Son at day break, her Mother assisted her.
Clear. I have been at home. Sarah Neal Came here to weav. at home.

X. Birth mr Craiges Son, Rheubin Branards allso Son allso. XX. Clear. I Came home and was Calld by mr Craige to See his wife who was in labour, & was Deld of her first Child, a Son, at 10h Evn and is as Cleverly as Could be Expected. I was Calld from there to See the wife of Rheubin Branard who was Delivd of her first Son & 4th Child before my arival. a t Cap t Huseys, Conys & Branards. Birth s 2nt and 3d. Asa Cumings s remains interd this aftern.

Clear and pleast. I Combd flax and wint to the hook for Corn but got none. I bot 28 lb rice and an ounce turnip Seed of Cap t Fillabrown, cos t 8/3. I Calld at the mos t of y e Stores, walkt there and home again. Calld to See Cap t Welts who is Confind by a wound in his thigh. at the hook. I was unwell in the night.
Clear. mr Hodges workt here, his wife here on a visit. I have hatchild 11 lb 3 oz flax. Sally washt, Dolly Spining Linning. a t home. mrs s Hodges was here.

# Clear and Extreamly Pleasan t . mr Barnabas Lambart and our Dag t Dolly were united in y e Bonds of matrimony (may it pleas the Author of Mercie to Bless them by restoreing her impared health and granting them long to liv in lov and harmony.) the Magistrate who performd was W m Brooks Esqr, he returnd y e fee to my Dagt. other attendants on the ocation were mr Capin, Sons Pollard & Jon a , mr Gill & y r wives, mr Pitts & Sister, mr Rich d Foster and wife, mr Stodert & mis s Pollard, together with all my famely which Consisted of mr Ballard, my Self, Son s Cyrus, Ephm, S. G. Moore, Sally Cocks, Lucy Petty & Bickford. mr Davenport ocationally was at Tea with us. the whole was performd with Desency. a t home. mr Barnabas Lambart and our Daugt Dolly were united in matrimony. mr Capin writing here fore n .

X. Birth of mr Danys Son & Daug t XX. Clear and warm. I workt on Some raggs for Coverled, and was Calld by mr Dany to go and See his wife who was in Labour. I left home after 3h pm. Shee was Deld at 4 of a Son and a t half after 4 of a Daughter. mother and infants are likely to do well. I receivd 6/ in part of my fee, and walkt home after Sun Sett. an informd that the Neighbours have Put up a Camp for mrs Peterson on the 8 rods road, back of mr Kenaday. a t mr Danys. Birth 30 th & 31 st . I receivd 6/.

Clear and warm. mr Ballard went to See his meddow, finds it as good as ever he Saw it. I have been at home. Sally Cocks is 20 years old this day. Son Town & Cyrus left here aft Breakfast. Sally piqu t 3 pints of rasburies this aftern. At home. Cyrus Slept here las t night. Son Town & he took Breakfs t .

A Severe Snow Storm, wind N E. Ephm & Jason Livermore junr returnd with hay from Josiah Hawls. one of Jon a s Oxen faild by thee way and they were oblidgd to leave him in the road. I have been at home. at home. Esq r Cony returnd from Coart yesterday.

Clear & Pleast. mr Ballard at Esq r Conys. Dagt Ballard and her Child n went home. Dolly and Sally washt & went to mr Gills. mr Princ Baker here. I was Calld to See Cap t Molloys infant, it is unwell. we gave it Rhubarb. I tarried all night. a t Capt Molloys. Dagt Ballard went home, Son Town Slept here.

Cloudy, raind aftern. I was Clearing my Door yd fore n . Asa Mason gave [me] an order on mr Baxter for what he owd me. he Dind here. Cap t Nichols & Son Alaxander here directly from Oxford, my friends are well. they bro t a Letter from Josiah Kingsbury. I wrote to Brors Collins Moore, Barton & Waters. a t home. wrote to my friends at the wes t . Dolly Sett a w b of Diaper to work.
Clear. I have been a t home quilling, Doing house work &C. Ephm has been makeing a Cheese press. a t home.

I Came from Cap t Husseys this morn, went with Son and Dagt Town to Son Pollards af t n. Patty Easty here to have an over Coat Cutt. Sally Cox & Ephm went to Singing School at Evn g . a t Ditoes & Son Pollards.
rainy. I was at mr Getchels, his wife is very unwell. a t mr Getchels.
Clear and warm. I returnd from Boltons. Calld at John Jonses, mr Lambarts and Son Pollards. I was very much fatagud. a t Ditoes. mr Sheppard, Robbins and 2 Gentlemen from Boston here.

Clear. Dagt Lambert wrode here. Sally was at y e fort and hook. I have Done work about house and mended Cloaths. mr Ballard has houghd Corn and Potatoes, Ephm allso. Cyrus here. at home. Dagt Lambart here, Cyrus allso.

Clear and very warm. Polly workt for her Self, Sally made a ha t for her. I was Calld to See Son Jonas inft, it Seems to have a Suden Cold. I gave her Rhubarb for it. mrs s Kimbal there on a visit. my Side was in Shech pain I found it Dificult to wride. I Calld to See my Dagts, they were well as usual. mr Ballard returnd. Polly went to Son Pollards. a t Son Jonas & my Dagts.

D Clear & very warm. I attended worship aftrn. mr [no entry] of winslow performd. his Discoarse was in vindication of a Teacher being Sent from God. I Calld to See Wilm Kenadys wife who has Cut her foot, Doct Cony there, Says her toe must be taken of. Ephm went to Jonas, finds them better. A t meeting, at mr Kenadys. Augus ts Ballard here.

G Clear foren n , raind before night. Selvester G. Moore here, made me a present of an Orange & 2 apples. mr Ballard & Sons went to meeting, I did not feel able to go. Cyrus Sleeps here. a t home. Selvister G. Moore here.
D a very rainy Day. I was a t mr Getchels, his wife unwell yet. a t Ditoes.
D Clear. I went to meeting foren n , was unwell aftern. my famely all attended Except Ephm. a t meeting.

G Clear. mr Ballard, my Self & Ephm attended Divine Service at y e fort. I went to mr Lambarts at intimition. he and mr Gill and their wives here after meeting. Sally went home with my Dagt to help make mr Ballard a Seut of Cloaths. mr Ezra Smiths wife a meeting aftern. at meeting. mr Gill [&] Lambart & y r wives here. mrs Smith a t Meeting.

Clear. I have been to Son Jonas, they are well; to mr Craigs, Shee is Comfortable; at mr Burtuns, Shee made me a presen t of a Shaul and paper pins; at mr Baxters, Shee is as well as Shee has been of late. I Drank Tea with old Lady Pollard and Spen t Evng with Davids wife. Dagt Town & Pollard there. mrs Barton here to have a goun made. a t Son Jon a s & other Neighbours.

Birth X Edmond Getchels Son. XX. Clear and pleasant Except Some what Cool. mrs s Getchell had her women Calld aftern and was Safe Deld of a Son at 4h pm. I left her and infant as well as Could be Expected at 7. Calld to See mr Fletchers Child, find it a little more Comforlable. a t Ditoes. Birth 24th. receivd 6/7.
Cloudy part of the day. I have Done my hous work & wed Gardin Some. Polly has Spun her Days work and helpt me Some about my work. At home.

Clear and warm. mr Ballard has been Surveying for mr Saml Cumings. I have workt in y e Gardin and tended the Cloath and Done house work. Sally Came home at Evn. Ephm workt on the road between here and mr Hodges. Lucy Kaneda brot home 9 Skeins Lining y n for which I paid by 1 peck flax Seed 1/6, Cash 2/8. a t home. Sally Cocks Came home at Evn fm mr Lamb ts .
Clear and very warm morn. I feel a little more Comfortable, have been at home. my Dagt Lambart here, Sally went home with her. Shee wrode our hors. a t home. Dagt Lam bt here.

Clear part of y e Day and very warm. Ephm and John Savage Sett out to go to the meddow. mr Ballard went up to y e fort. mrss Capin and my Dagt and mr Luke Lambart here, they have been to y e hook. Sally Cocks went home with them. a t home. mr Ballard and my Self only Slept in this house y s night.

Clear Excep t a Shower. mr Benjamin Calld me to See his Child, it Expird at 4h pm before that I reacht there. mrs s Mosier and my Self Put on the grave Cloaths and I Came away. Calld to See mr Burtuns Child, it is mending. I Calld on my Dagts and mr Gill as I wen t . the Latter is unwell. mr Ballard been to mr Whites and to a Parrish Meeting. at mr Benjamins, his Child Expird this afternoon.
rainy. I have been at home. the hoop of my Soap Barrels broke and let my Soap out. I had a great fatague in geting it up; los t a good deal. at home. James Moore, his wife is unwell.
A Snow Storm, Cleard of at Evng. I have been at home. Ephm hurt his nose this morn. a t home.
Cloudy. mr Ballard, Silva & mr Morril gone to Survey at Bodinham. I have been at home. we began to quillt a Bed quillt for Dolly [at ]. at [ ].

Clear and very pleasant. mr Ballard, my Self, Selva & Ephm went to meeting a t the Acadima. a Discourse Deld by Revd mr Bradford of Pownalboro adapted to the ocation, viz, the opening the Semenary of inslruction. Deacon Coney and Lady Dind with us and I went to mr Livermrs with them. Jonas wife & Children Came here. the house he built for Bizer Benjamin to liv in was Consumd by fire las t Evng. a t meeting a t y e hook and at mr Livermores.
Clear. I have houghd my things by y e house and my Gardin this Day, feel Some falagud. Ezra Town Came here at Evn. Betsy Page was here. at home. Ezra Town Sleeps here.

Clear. mr Ballard went to meddow, Sally Cocks to Son Lambarts. we were informd of the Death of her Brother John, who diseas t at Jamaca on June 19th, 1795. I have been at home, workt very har d . at home. Sally a t mr Lambarts. Shee tarried all night.

Clear and very warm. mr Ballard went to the Meddow, the men finisht mowing there. Old Lady Bullin and mr T. Pages wife Came here on a visit foren. mr Ph p Bullin Came and took Tea, and Conducted y m home. a t home. mrs Bullin & Page here. mr Ballad at the Meddow.
Clear and warm. mr Piter Clearks wife Came to See mrs Molloy. at Ditoes.

X. Birth Jessy Kimbals Dagt, first Born. XX. rainy. I was Calld from mr Blacks to Capt Cocks s to See his Dagt Kimbal a t 4h morn. Shee was very ill but was Safe Deld at 4h pm of a fine Daugh t . I left her and infan t as well as Could be Expected and returnd home by water; was Some fatagud. our famely were in Bed w n I Came [home]. a t Capt Cocks s . Birth 23 d . receivd 6/ as a reward.

Cloudy and Sun Shine by turns foren, a heavy Shower about noon, rain afterwards the most of y e Day & Evn. I houghd my Water & musk mellons, quilld and Carded Cottne. mr Cooshman was Ordaind a t Winslow this Day. a t home. Ephm gone to Ordination.

Clear and warm. I went to the hook, paid mr Josep h Dummer 15 Dolrs for a Buro, Table and Bd Stead for Dolly. I Bot of mr Strattne 2 poringers @ 1/9, one 1/6 - 5/ -, 2 Candlesticks 1/4; at Rol d Smiths 2 Kenisters 2/8, the whole 9/. I went to See mrs Waid whose Oldest Child or Dag t Expird a t 10h this morn. mr Ballard has been Surveying for mr Child and Patridg. Ephm Bro t Dollys furniture from mr Dumers. a t the hook and a t mr Waids. his Dag t Betsy Expird y s morn a t 10h .

Clear and hott. mrs Webber Spun 16 knotts of Cottne for me. I feel very much Jard with the fall I had yesterday, but keep about. I had a fine meSs of String beens y s day. at home. this was the morn mrs Peterson was Deld.

Clear. I have been at home. mrss Gill & Abbot walkt here, their husbands Came and Conducted them home by water. mr Ballard went to Survey [lines] for mr White yesterday and returnd to Day. Sarah Neal Came to weave for [me]. at home. mr Gill & Abbot & their wives here.

Snowd. I returnd to mr Craigs a t 4h morn, wen t to bed and Slep t a little. I Came home aftern, find Dagt Ballard and Childn here, mrs Foster and her infant allso. mr Town & Ezra Sleep here. I do feel fatagud. a t Dittoes Craigs and Brainards.

Clear. mr Lambart and wife went to Dine at Son Pollards, and Spend aftern a t mr Capins. mr Capin has been writing on Plans for mr Ballard, he bestowd 6/ as a reward for my assisting his wife. a t home.

Clear and warm. I workt Some in my Gardin and Some on raggs for Coverld. mr James [Webber] and his Sister Sally Dind here. John Kenady & David Densmore workt here, had 9/ in Cash at night. mr Shubal Hinkleys man workt aftern. Sally Shows much uneasiness about her wages. magr Stickney here, receivd money of mr Ballard as a Bounty for a wolf. at home. magr Stickney receivd a Bounty for wolf of mr Ballard.
Cloudy, Some rain aftern. Sally washt. I houghd my Gardin and Got String beens for Densmr. mr Ballard wrode out on business. a t home. [Nancy Young] a Son, [but] expird y e 25.

Clear and windy. I left y e infant a little more Comfortable. mrs s Molloy made me a present of muSlin for a Cap. I felt Dull after I Came home. mr Preskott & Son & mr Pullin Dind here. I Sowd Seeds for Sallit. a t Dittoes.
rainy all day. I have been Doing a little in y e Door y d & kniting. Dolly wove 2 yds of her Diaper. at home.

Clear. I have been at home. Dagt Ballard and Jason Pierce s wife Dind here. I gatherd String Beens, abou t 3 pints. I was Calld out of Bed a t 10h Evn to go and See mr Pillsburys Child, its Complaint was obstruction of urin. I recommended giving a little Niter which relievd the Patient. a t mr Pillsbury s in ye night.

D Clear & pleasant. my famely, Except my Self and Dolly, went to meeting at y e hook. mrss Holman, mr Trask & wife, August s Ballard, Jessy Bullin, W m Parmer, Anna Bullin and Fanny Cocks Supt here. the Girls Sleep here, Ballard allso. a t home. receivd a Letter from Doc t Barton of April 22 nt , by mrss Holman.

D Clear Except a Showr in ye morn. the Child Seemd Cleverly and I Calld in to See mrs Smith, find her better than Shee has been. I went to meeting, mr Ballard & Son Town returnd from Coart this morn and attended, Cyrus up allso. we wint to mr Lamberts at intermistion. he and wife with mr Capin & his and mr Town Came after Meeting, Dind and took Tea with us. a t mr Pillsburys and a t meeting.

Cloudy and Some rain. mr Ballard went to pittst n . I have planted Cucumbers and Sow d Turnip and Lettuce Seed. mrs s Page here, had Gardin Seeds. Polly Livermore here at Evn. Rich d Foster here, his famely are well. Bro r Ebinz Moore, Anna & Fanny Sleep here. at home. Bro r Moore Slept here.

Showers from morn to Evn when we had a heavy one. I workt between Showers at mooving the Doore Dirt. Dolly Cut her web out after weaving 4 yds & Drew it for plain Cloath, it being So hard to weav Shee Could not weav it. a t home.
raind. mr Town left here homewd bound. I have felt very dul. Sally Extracted a tooth for me. I have Carded Cottne, wound quills and Sett Cabbage plants. Ephm began to mow our grass. a t home.

X. Birth Jery Badcks Son. XX. Clear and pleast. I was Calld by Ben n Petingal to go to Jerimy Badcoks. went to y e fort by water, was very wet when I arivd there. was taken on hors back there, wrode more than a mile then orderd to return, y e patient being Deld of a Son. I Crost the river in y e Boat after receiving 6/ of mr Pet ngll for my troubl. I was invited to take Breakfast with mr Smith. I Dind there allso. took Tea at mr Gills.
