Clear and Cold. I was Calld at Evn to See mr Theops Hamlin who has been unwell this ten days. I Came home at 9 h 30m, Sett up to work till after midnight. at mr Theops Hamlins, his paren t s there.
Clear. mr Ballard to work at Son Jonas. I have been at home. a t home.

A rainy, quite windy at Evn g . mr Ballard and I attended worship, the ordinanc of y e Supper was administred. I Came to Son Lambarts at intermition, my Dagt is not very well. Cyrus Came home this aftern. at meeting.
Cloudy, Some Snow at Evn. mr Ballard workt on the Barn. I have been at home. Hepsy and I Botomed 4 Chairs with old Leather. at home. botomed Chairs.
Cloudy and Some rain. Son Town left here after Breakfast. mr Ballard been makeing a plan for Jessy Blunt. Hepsy went to mr Dingleys. at home.
Clear. I have been at home, Cleand the Store & Spoold my Dagt Lambarts warp. Son Jona here, brot his OXen to pasture. Hepsys Bror John Sleeps here. a t home. Son Town left here.

X. Birth Wilm Swantons Son. X. Clear. Cyrus went to mr Hamlins and receivd 6 lb Butter on mr Gills acount. Son and Dagt Lamb t , with their Child n Supt with us and Spen t y e Evn g . I was Calld at 7h Evn to See mrs s Swanton. Son Lambar t Conducted me to Stanleys in a Sleigh. mr Swanton took me there, his wife was Safe Deld at 9 h 30 m of her Second Son and 4 th Child. I Came home before I Slep t . Son Town & wife Sleep here. [a t mr Swantons. Birth 2 nt . Sep t 3, 1798. I receivd 6/ by Dagt Pollard.]

Clear morn. mr Ballard went and borrowed Esqr Davis s Sleigh and Conducted me to See mr Pattin whome I find very Low indeed. I tarried all night; he had a very restless night. [Juda] Smith Sett up with me to watch. mrs s Pattin is as well as Can be Expected. at mr Pattins, he is [very] low [indeed]. Old mr Hinkley Disceast this Evn.

X. Birth Son Lambart Dagtr. XX. rainy, it was this morn, not yesterday. I wen t to mr Lambarts, Shee was ill all day, her women were Calld in the Evn g and Shee Safe Deld at 8 h 45 m of a fine Dag t . her Second Child. Dagt Pollard tarried all night. at Son Lambarts. Birth 51 st . recd 12/.
Snowd part of the Day, Clear and very Cold at night. we had the women Calld to attend mrs s Moore, Shee was ill till 3h mrn. at Ditoes. the women were Calld at 9h Evn.

A X. Birth James Childs Son. XX. rainy and froze. I was Calld from Son Lambar ds at 3h y s morn to See mrs s Child who was in Labour. Shee was Safe Delivird at 6 h 45 m of a fine Son, her fifth Child. I left her Cleverly at 10 and returnd to Son Lambards, tarrie with them this night. at Ditoes & mr Childs. Birth 52 nt . Decr 1802 recd 10/6 of mr Child for performing y s [ ].

A X. Birth James Moores Second Son. XX. Clear and Cold. mrs s Moore was Safe Deld at 3h morn of a Son, her 8 th Child. Shee has been very ill, but I left her more Comfortable at 10 hours morn, reacht home about 2 pm. Old mr Lambar t and Barna, and my Dagt & son Ephm here at Evn. the old Gentleman Sleeps here. at Ditoes. Birth 54. I receivd 9/ as a reward.Hepsy was 18 tn years old yesterday.

G X. Birth James Hinkleys 2 nd Son & 3 Child. XX. Cloudy. mr Ballard and Cyrus are gone to meeting. Cyrus Came home at noon, it raind. James Hinkley Came a t 1 h pm for me to go and See his wife who is in Labour. we arivd, find his mother and Ben Churches wife there. we Calld the m c Causland wives after Sun Sett & Patient was Deld at 7h Evn of a fine Son, her 3 d Child, and is Cleverly. I tarried all night. at James Hinkleys, New France. Birth 10th. recd 1_ bushl Corn the 4 ins t .

very rainy morn. I was Calld a t 25m after midnight to See the Lady of mr James Child who was very unwell thro y e day. I tarried with her thro this night. Shee Seemd more Comfortable a t 10h Evn. at mr Childs, his wife is very unwell.

Clear and very pleas t . I tarried with my Dagt Lambard till Evn, then Came home, Ephm with me. mr Capin Sleeps there. Patty & Nabby Livermore here at Evng. mr Ballard Sett out this day to go to view land between this river and penobscott, with a man by the name of Bond, he is from Vermo nt State. at Son Lambarts. my Dagt is very Comfortable.
Clear and Cold. mr Ballard wen t to the hook for Salt. I have been at home, Hepsy washt. at home.

Cloudy, raind Some. I left my patient as well as Could be Expected and returnd home at 10h m. mr Ballard & Cyrus are gone to Town meeting, it was Put by by reason of the warning being illegal. I went to bed and Slept Some. Snowd at Evn g . at James Hinkleys.
Clear till near night. we Scolt my pickls, Bak t , pared and Cut apples. Cyrus and Hoit dug our Beets. I have been at home. at home.

Cloudy. mr Ballard been Sleding wood. mr Dingly & Hilton help t him Chop. I have made 26 & _ DZ n Candles, it is the last Tallow we have that Came ou t of the Cow we killd a year ago. at home. made 26_ Dozn Candles.

Clear part of the Day, Snowd at Evn. mr Ballard went to Son Jonas, he and his Son Jack, Son & Dag t Lambar t and Child n & Rachel Capin here a t Evn g . I have See 63 years this day. [at home], My Birthday.

Clear. mr Ballard went to Col o Norths, Conducted me to Son Pollards to See their Babe. I find it better. I Came home a t Evn. Cyrus informs that Le faett Seems more Comfortable. I Calld at Son Lamb ts , they are well. at Son Pollards & Lamb ts .

X. Birth [Nath n ] Shaws 3 d Dagt. XX. Cloudy the most of the day, Some Snow. I do not feel very well but attended at the hous of the widdow Sewall on her husbands funeral. Son Jona brot his two oldest Sons here. Cyrus went up there, Carrid Le faett home. Sons Jona and Lambart are gone to Conduct W m and Levy Parmer, John and Freeman Hinkley before EbenZ Farewell for Tryal for their asaulting John Johnson and Rufus Ballard on the high road. I was Calld at 7h Evn by mr Shaw to See his wife, Shee was Safe Deld a t 8 of a Dagt, her 5th Child and is Cleverly. I tarried all night. I had a fit of the night mair. at the funeral mr Moses Sewall, and at mr Shaws, hook. Birth 9 th . recd 7/6 as a reward.
Snowd the most of the day. mr Ballard went to a Lecture at Hallowell. I have been very unwell this day. a t home.
Reuben Fairfield and Selva Hatch here. mrs Gillman and mrss Hinkley here on a visit. at home, had Company.

Clear. I returnd home from Seps Early this morn, fatagud, went to bed and Slept. mr Ballard Sett out to Survey for Friend Fairfield. Hepsy went to Spend y e night at James Hinkleys. mrs s Whitwell & Cragge were here after I left home yesterday. at Sieps. mr Ballard went [to mr ] Fairfields.
Cloudy, windy. mr Ballard went to Son Jon a s and mr Baleys and Hon Danl Conys. my little Le faett is no better. I have been piecing a Bed Cover & knitt some. at home.
Clear. mr Ballard gone up Streat. I feel very unwell. James Hinkley bro t me 1_ bushels Corn for my assisting his wife. at home. mr James Hinkly brot me 1_ bushel Corn.

X. Birth mr Gows Son. XX. Clear and very pleas t . I was Calld at 1h 40m in morn to See mrs s Gow. Shee was Safe Deld at 4h 40m of a fine Son, her 3 d Child. I left them Cleverly and returnd at 11 h . mrs Moore Calld here. I went to see mrs Dingly. at mr Gows. Birth 46th. recd 9/ as a reward.
raind. I Came home at 2h 30m pm, left the Patient more Comfortable. mr Ballard is gone to Son Jonas. a t Ditoes.

Clear. this Day is of observd as A day of Thanksgiving. I did not attend worship, my Childn who resede here were to Super with us Excep t Moses Pollard who rather Chose other Company. his wife & Childn and Lucy Shaw were oblidged to tarrie. at home. my Child n to Supper with me, my Dagts & Jonas wife Sleep here.
Clear and not So cold. mr Ballard wen t to work at my Sons. I have been at home. Phillip Bullin here at Evn. Rob t Fletcher left us after Breakfas t . at home.

Clear. this day is Sett aPart as a day of Fasting and Prayer. mr Ballard, Cyrus and my Self attended worship at Augusta. the Revd mr Stone Discoarsd from Prov s 14th Ch 34 th vers. I went to mrs s Pattins at intermition. at meeting, wrode in a Sleigh. Calld at Son Lamb ts .
Cloudy and Some Snow. mr Ballard went to work on the Barn a t Son Jonas. I have been at home. Ephm Sleeps here. at home.
Clear. mr Ballard and Son Ephm brot 10 h Hay from mr Spragus. I have been at home. Es qr Preskot and mr Bishop Dind here. we had 9 lb Butter of y e Es qr on Cred t . at home.

Cloudy part of the day. Cap t Osgood here, gave a mr Barrow a deed of land. mr Ballard and my Self were witness to y e Sineing and delivery. Son Jona Came here for me to go and See his two youngest Childn who are Sick. this is 20 years Since I firs t Set my feet on the Kenebeck Shore, it was at mr John Jones s Landing below the hook. at Son Jon a s, his Child n are unwell. it is 20 years this day Since I landed at mr John Jones s where I Spent 1 year and 17 days, then removd to his mill at Bomans Brook.

Clear. mr Gill & Lamb t , with their wives and Babes, Came here and joind Company with Son Town & his wife and went to pittst n on a visit to Bro r Towns. mr Gill paid me 6/ Cash. mr Ballard gone to y e fort. I returnd from mr Swantons at 2h 30m y s morn. the widdow Savage & mrs Rachel there; they are unwell. [at Ditoes at 2h morn.]

Clear. I was Calld from mr Pattins about 10h m by mr Eben zr Church to go and See the wife of Samuel Davis. I find her unwell but not in labour. I tarried all night, mrs s Brown allso. I had but little rest by reason of the Cold and hard lodging. at Ditoes & Saml Daviss.

Clear. Phillip Bullin dind here. mr Ballard went to Parish meeting. we go t a kettle Soap into the Seller. I finish t kniting a pair hoes for mr Ballard, the 4 P r I have made him this winter. at home. Nath l Whicker Expird.
G rainy. I have been at home. mr Bald & Cyrus at [meet ng ]. a t home.
A rainy. Hoit only, of my famely, attended Divine Service. I have been at home. mr Ballard went to See John Davis Eqr. at home. Son Ephm & Ebn Cole Dind here. Thankful here.
Clear. mr Ballard and Son Town went to meeting. mr Town went home at Evng. mr Gill bro t Lucy Town and his little Son here; Came for him after meeting. Lucy tarries with me. a t home.
Cloudy. mr Ballard Sett out for Eas t Andiver at 7h this morn. I have been at home. at home.
Clear. mr Ballard went to winth p . I have been ["a t home" - implied]. I Finisht makeing SoaP and began a Stockin for Cyrus. a t home.

Cloudy morn, Snowd at 10h morn. I was at mr Hamlins Store, left mrs s Child near night. find the roads very bad. Calld at Son Lambards, tarried there all night. a t ditoes; at mr Hamlins and Son Lambarts.
Clear and very pleast. I have been at home till Evng, was then Calld to See mrss Child who had an ill turn. I took Care of her thro the night. a t mr Childs.
Clear and very Cold. mr Ballard went to work at Son Jonas, he informs me the Child n are unwell. I have been at home. a t home. it is the aniversary of my marriage and 43 years.
Clear. I have been at home. the ice Broke away at our Shoar this Evng and up at the fort. at home.

Birth Wil m Whites Son. rainy and Snow morn. I was Calld abou t 6 hours to Moses Parmers to See his wife at 7, from there to Wm Whites. Calld back to Parmers at 3h pm. mrs s White Calld mrs s Hinkley and was Deld at 4 of a Son. a t Moses Parmers and Wil m Whites. Snowd this Morn.
Clear. I have been at home, washt the west room. mr Ballard & Cyrus to hear Coart. Son Town Sleeps here. at home. Son Town Sleeps here.
Clear. I have been at home overhauling my wolen pieces for a Bed Cover. at home.

Clear. Colo North Calld here, he and mr Ballard are gone to woolwidg. I have been at home, mended my winter goun and knit Some. Cyrus Came from Jonas, Le faett is better. a t home. mr Bald to woolwidg. Snowd all night.

Clear and pleast. I returnd home at 7 h this morn, left my patient Cleverly. mr Ballard is gone up to the uper Parrish on business, he was hearing Coart aftern. at mr Shaws. mr Johnson & Ballard Sleep here.

Clear and pleasant. mr Finney and Crosby helpt us git wood. mr Ballard went to Son Jonas. mrs Dingley here at Evng. I have been Sewing raggs. Hepsy finisht making me a Cottne Shift, her father dind here. friend Fairfield here aftern. at home. mrs s Dingley here at Evng.
Clear.I have been at home. Hepsy & I put a Bed Cover into the fraim and quilted it out. at home.
raind. I have been Sewing & kniting. mr Ballard been to mr Balys to Seek for hay. Jonas Son is no better. mr Soal Sent me 9/. at home.
Clear bu t Cold. mr Ballard giting wood. Cyrus went to pitts tn to tend mill for mr Moore. Jacky is gone to School. I find my Self very lonesome. Phillip Bullin been here y s aftern. at home.
Clear and Cold. mr Lambart Butcherd a pigg which w d 70 w t for us. mr Ballard to work on the Barn at Son Jonas. I have been at home, began a Shift for my Self. at home. we had a pigg Butcherd.

Clear. moses Came for his famely, Son Lamb t for his, Jona for his. they all took Breakfast with us and retired from here. Son and Dagt Lambar t to Varsalboro, Jack and L e faett Ballard Came here. Augustus Ballard & Th l Godfray Sleep here. at home. my Child n went from here after Breakfas t . Hepsy at mr Hinkley ys Evng.
Clear. mr Ballard workt at my Sons. I have been at home. a t home.
at Seth Pitts & cowins.
Clear Except a Shower. I have been very unwell this day, but had my work to do. mrss White of Hallowell here aftern. at home, mrs s White here.

Clear. mr Ballard went to Son Jona s , Son Ephm to work here. mr Carter, his wife and mr Burtun Came here, tarried but a little while. I have been at home. Hepsy washt the rooms. at home. mr Carter & wife here.
Clear. mr Ballard makeing plan and went to Hallowell. I have done my house work. Watson diging the gardin. Shubal Pitts s wife Calld here. at home.
A Clear. I have been at home, feel very unwell. Cyrus Came up, mr Ballard and he went to meeting at Augusta, Watson to Hallowell. a t home.
A Severe Snow Storm. Ephm went home after noon. mr Ballard unwell with a Soar throat. we Did not attend public worship, any of us. I Shoveld a path round the house after the Storm was over. a t home.

Clear. I tarrid with my Patient till near night when I left her more Comfortable. am informd that the remains of mr Hinkly were interd this Evn and that mr Pattin deceast this morn. Hepsy went to the hook after I Came home. [ at Saml Davis. Death mr Pattin. Internment of old mr Hinkley.]

Cloudy, Snowd Some aftern. Son Pollard Came here, brot his two oldest Dagts. he Conducted Dagt Town and her two Dagts to his house. Son Town, his wife and Babe and mrs s Trask Dind and took Tea with us. mr Ballard been to Jona s & y e fort. I have been at home. at home. Dagt Town went [home]. Son [ ] & wife Came here.

Clear. mr Ballard wen t to Son Jon a s, Watson went and bro t a Swine from here and went to mill. I have washt, feel much fatagud. Thom s Hinkleys wife here, the Widdo Davis Sleeps here. at home. wido Davis Sleeps here, had gardin Seeds of me.
Clear. Dagt Lambart Came here, Shee tarries all night, Ephm allso. I have been at home. a t home. Dagt Lambart Sleeps here.
very Cold. I have been with my Dagt Lambart, we made 20
Clear and Cold. I have been at home. mr Ballard bot a piece Cloath for Hepsy. at home.

Cloudy. mrss Densmore here to warp a web. mrss Dingley and Nancy here. the river Opend more but Slopt below our Shore. mr Ballard returnd from winthroy, informs that the Bridg over Bomans brook is moved. at home. mrs s Densm r & Dingley here.

raind the most of the day. mrs Smith went home aftern. mr Ballard went to Sidny to Seek Some Girl to help me, but did not Suckseed. Watson Cleand my Seller. I have done my house work. at home. mrs Smith went home.
Cloudy. mr Ballard & Cyrus a t Court. Old mr Hopkins Sleeps here, John Brown allso. I have been at home. at home. mr Hopkins Sleeps here.

Snow, haill and almost rain. I have been to work on my bed Cover, Hepsy allso. mr Ballard been to Son Jonas and mr Burtuns, the latter Setled acounts with him. the Ballance in mr Ballards favr, five Dollars and 14 Cents for wich he has taken a note Sind Eustis & Burtun. a t home.
Snowd and blowd all day. I have mended mr Ballards thik Sett waistcoat. at home.

Clear and pleasant. mr Bullin and his wife dind and took Tea with us. mr Ballard and Cyrus went up to the Coart house to hear a Cause tried. the Hinkleys and Parmers were acquited. at home. mr Bullin & wife here.

Clear. mr Ballard wen t to Varsalboro to Survey for mr Webber. he and Watson washt our Sheep before he Set out. Watson went to work for my Son . Dagt Pollard and Jon a s wife & Jack Came here aftern. Dagt Ballard and Jack & Ephm tarried all night. mrss Livermore here foren. I have had a Sick aftern. at home. mr Ballard gone to Varsalboro. my Dagts Pollard & Ballard here, mrss Livermore allso.

Cloudy morn, Snowd before noon. mr Ballard and Crosby got home 3 loads of wood at 4h pm and Crosby went home. old mrss Kenny here, Eat dinner. I have been Sewing old pieces of Cloath together. Hepsy making me a Shift. at home.
Cloudy, snowd before night. we have been piecing a border for a bed Cover and put it on. Cyrus went to his Brors. I finisht a pair of hoes y s Evn for mr Ballard. at home.

Clear and pleasant. mr Ballard Surveying & makeing a plan for mr Vauhn. I have been Doubling thread and piecing raggs. Hepsy went to mr Hinkleys to twist some thread. Shee went to mrs Gitchels and receivd 6/ of her in part for my assisting her in Child Birth. at home.
Clear. I have been at home, mr Ballard to his Sons, Jack to School. I washt the N. Bedroom and wes t room. at home.

X. Birth Thoms Kenneys Dagt. XX. Clear. mrss Baxter here. I have been kniting. I was Calld at 8h 30m Evng to See the wife of Thoms Kenney who was in Labour and was Safe Deld at 10 h 30 m of a Dag t . I tarried all night, my Patient as well as Could be Expected. this is her 6th Child. at Thom s Kenneys. Birth 13th. receivd9/ July 11th by Son Cyrus.
Clear and very windy. I have been at home, have no help. Polly left me yesterday. I paid her 4/ for her Service and gave her a Small Looking glass. at home, one of my Ducks began to Sett.
Clear. Augs t & Thankfull went from here after Diner. I have been at home, we Bak t Pudings & Pies. Jack Ballard Seemd very unwell last night but is Cleverly to day. at home.

Clear part of the Day, a little Snow after. Son Jona Came and Conducted his Sons and Dorkis and Allin Lambar t from here. mr Ballard been to his hous to work. I have been at home, finisht my Self a Cottne Shift. I footed a pair hoes for Jack, knitt hands to a pair mitts for my husband and nurs t the Childn. at home. Dorkis and the little Boys went home.
a t Savage Boltons & Cowens.

Clear part of the day. men here on business with mr Ballard. I have got thro my work with great inebility. we had thunder and rain aftern. Watson Sowd beets and Parsnips. I Sent Dagt Lambart 9 Ducks Eggs. at home, very unwell.

A Clear and pleast. Cyrus Came up, mr Ballard went to meeting foren. Cyrus Came with Jona s hors and Sleigh and Conducted me to Son Lambarts from there. I wrode with them to meeting. Son Jona Came and brot me home. at meeting. the Tex t Prov bs [ ] 23 v .

G Clear and very warm. we all attended worship. Cyrus was up. the Ordinance of the Supper Administred. mr Stone Discoarst from 2 Corinth foren and from 1st Corinth aftern. Pegy Crage Desired our Prayers being near the Close of Life!!! my Dagts and Sons wife here after meeting. at meeting. Sacrament day.
Clear. I was at home. at home.
at Cowens. 179

Birth mr Pattins Dag t . Clear. Sons Pollard & Lambart Came for me to go and See mrs s Pattin who was in Labour at 7h morn. our Sleigh over Set before we arivd, but we were not hurt. the Patient was Safe Deld abou t 8 of a Dagt. I left her Comfortable at noon. mr Pattin is yet alive but very low. we had the Company of Sister Pollard. all my Child n that are living by Birth and Affinity (which make 10 in number) toger with mr Gill & Pitts & their wives and Babes to Dine and take Tea with us. Sister Pollard tarries all night. Son & Dagt Town went to mr Gills. at mr Pattinns till near noon. my Childn and someother friends Dind and took Tea with us. Birth 3 d .may God Bless and prosper my Dear Child n . if weShould never meet Again in y s life, may we meet togetherin a future.
Clear and Cold. I have been at home, mr Ballard with a Bridg Committee, Cyrus to mill. I feel unwell at night. at home.
Clear and warm. mr Ballard and Watson workt at Son Jon a s. I have been at home. Nabby and I went in to the field and gathered Strawberes after we finisht our washing. at home.

Clear and hoter than it has been this Sumer. mr Ballard been to Town meeting and to Genl Sewals. Nabby Washt and went to mr Densmores. Son and Dagt Town Came here at 7h Evn, my Dagt is feeble but not more So than I Expected to See her. may it please God to restore her to health, or prepare her for a happy futurity. mr Ballard informs me that Pegy Cragg Expired this aftern at the 6th hour. may we all attend to the Call and be ready. Watson Came home at 10 h 15 m . I have been at home, Sewd Some and houghd Cucumbers. at home. Son Town brot his wife here.
Clear. mr Ballard went to Son Jonas, little Epm has Scolt him in y e face and arm. I have been at home. at home.

Clear and very warm. mr Ballard and Watson wen t to Son Jon a s, his wife and my two Daugts here to See me, Dollys Child n allso. Polly Densmore here, allso. John Brown here, informd me his Mother Departed this life las t Fryday and her remains interd yesterday. may God Sanctify the berievment to her Surviveing Companion and her Dear Children. at home. mrs s Davis left here. my Dag ts here to See me. I was informd of the Death ofmrs s Brown. thunder this Evng.
a very rainy foren. I have been at home. Watson went to haull Some Boards from Colo Howards mill to the river. at home.

X. Birth Rach l Savage s Dagt. X. Clear. mr Ballard went to Son Jon a s. I was Calld to See Rachel Savage who was in Labour and was Deld at 6h pm of a Dead Child. I left her Cleverly and returnd home at 9h Evn. At wido Savages. Birth 14, recd 6/.

Clear and very warm. mr Ballard went to Pittst n to Survey for Doc t Parker & others. Son Town Sett out for home, his wife has had a very Sick day indead. mrs s Liverm r , Dingly & Page Calld to See her, mr Bond was here allso. Nabby went to the interment of mrs Crage. at home. Nursing my Dag t Town. Peggy Crage [interd].
Clear. I have been Nursing my Dag t Town. Shee has had a more Comfortable day than yesterd y , but Cought much at night. mr Ballard returnd aftern. Doct Cony Calld to See my Dagt y s morn. at home.
D zn Candles. at Ditoes & at Son Lambarts.
Clear and very Cold & windy. mr Ballard got a Load wood. I have been at home. [Hepsy at Hinklys].

rainy. the ice was Stopt below us and the Entervail Covered all over with water, our Shore fence Carrid away. I have been at home. mr Ballard went up to See the Bridges, that over Kenebeck Some Damaged, that over the brook impasable. at home.

very heavy Showers. mr Ballard and Watson went to Son Jonas, Jack wen t home. James Sturges Dind here, he & Derias Ballard here yesterday. mr Lambard informd that Dag t Town thinks her Self on the recovery. a t home.

X. Birth of a Son of John V. Davis, born by Mehitable Peirc, her firs t Born. XX. a rainy Day. I was Calld at the first hour to See mrs Mehettable Peirce who was in Labour, and was Safe Deld a t 7 h morn of a very fine Son which Shee Declared was begoton by John Varsal Davis Esqr. this Shee positively affirmd in the Extremity of her illness, in presenc of the wife of Jon a Ballard who with Hosea Houland were my assistants in the Case. the latter was Stept into an other room when I Examined my Patient. mrs s Tetcomb was Calld but did not arive till after the Birth of the Child. mrss Burtun and the mr Father of the Child Came to See them aftern. I receivd 24/ as a reward and returnd home. mr Ballard went up and acompanied me home. at Son Jonas. Birth 17 th . I receivd 24/ as a reward.

Clear morn. my Dagt Seems a little Easier than Shee was Las t night. I was Calld at 9h this morn to See mrs s Lowell who was in Labour. Shee had been attend ed by Doct Page last night; he was Calld home at Sunrise to his wife who was in Labour. Shee was Deld at 11h foren of a Son. the Doc t was Calld to my Patient and I returnd home at 4h pm. I receivd 9/ of mr Lowell. my Dagt Town has had a better day this day. Shee had but little feaver. mr Gill & Lamb d took Tea with us. at mr Lowells at Hallowell. find mrss Gill and Dagt Lamb t & her Child n here. Birth Doc t Pages Son.
Clear. I have been at home. mr Ballard & Cyrus been to Coart. a man by the name of Stephens Came here to work. a t home.

X. Birth mr Soals Dag t . XX. Clear and very pleasant. mr Soal Calld me to See his wife at 6h morn. Shee was Safe Deld at 2 pm of a fine Dagt, her Sixth Child. mrss Smith, Dickmn, Pitts and Dagt Lambart [assistants]. I returnd home at 6h Evn, left my patients Cleverly. my Son Ephm Came up from Dresden this day, he Calld here. at [home ]. [ ] 9/ as a reward.
Clear and pleast. I have been at home, mend a Cot for mr Ballard. he returnd from woolwich. a t home. mr Ballard Came home.
Clear and Spring like, the Snow waisted very much. mr Ballard Surveyed for John Brooks. Son Ephm Sleeps here, he is 19 years old this night at 11h. at home. my Son Ephm is 19 years old y s day.

G rainy. mr Ballard went to meeting, the ordinance of y e Super administred. I have been very unwell indead. Cyrus Came up from Pitts tn , he informs mrs s Mc Causland is better. at home, So unwell that I did not Sett up but little.

Cloudy, raind before night. I have felt more Comfortable. Son Jon a s wife here all day, did my work. my Son took Breakfs t & Diner with me. Watson workt for him, informs when he Came home that L e faett Scolt his foot this morn. Jona wen t home but it raind So that his wife and Child n tarry all night. at home. Dagt Ballard here this day and night. a 2 nd Duck began to Sett.

Clear part of the Day, a Shour aftern. my Dagt wrode as far as Tho s Hinkleys. Shee Seems fatagud, has not been So well all day. Son Jon a and wife here. mr Gill Came to bring Some Buckthorn roots for my Dagt. at home. Dagt Town is not So well y s Day. Jon a & wife to See her.

X Birth Timy Pages Dag t . XX. Clear and pleasant. I was Calld by Will m White at 4h 20m morn to go to See the wife of Tim y Page. we took Cap t Coxes Lady in at his house. the pateint Deld of a fine Dagt which w d 11 2/2 lb at 5h 50m. I was Conducted home by Leut Jessy Bullin where we arivd at 4h 30m pm. I left my Patients Cleverly. mr Page Sent mr Ballard 2/2 bushl wheat in part of what is due for Taxes. at Timothy Pages. Birth 6 th. Sept 28 th 1799 receivd 6/ in weaving Done by his wife.

Cloudy morn, Snowd aftern. Cyrus went up to the Settlement. mr Patradg Came for me to go to his house, but as I was Expecting imediate Calls from home I did not go. his infants Superfluous thumb was taken off this day by Doct Cony. Hepsy finisht our Bed Cover. I have been doing other matters. a t home. mr Patridg Came after me but I Did not go.
Clear and pleast. I have been at home, boxt my grea t wheal with Leather and Spun 14 knots of worsted. a t home. Spun worsted.
Cloudy and Some rain. I have been at home. mr Ballard went to y e hook to Lecture. 7 years Since we removd here.

Clear. I Came home by water, arivd a t 6h 30m morn. mr Abr m Devenport assisted to Conduct me there by water and home again. I do feel really fatagud with my Tour, wen t to bed at 10h and rose at 2h pm, feel much refreshed. Isaac Hardins wife here. at Ditoes. Isaac Hardins wife here.

Clear. I did my work, washt a Bedroom & the kitchen. mr Ballard and I attended at the funeral of the wife of Soloman Cumings. the procession was from the hous of her Brother Esq r Cony, a long Concoars of People attended. I feel much fatagud. my Son Ephm Sleeps here. at the interment of mrs Cumings.

Clear foren, Cloudy aftern. mr Ballard been to Pitts tn to the fort and to Hollowell. the Lady of Esqr Mathew Haywood, his Grand Dagt and Dagt Lambard here, James Moore allso. I have been at home. at home. am informd of the death of Cap t Zimr y Haywood, his remains interd this Day.

X. Birth mr Kitridgs Dagt. XX. Clear and warm. my Dagt Town Seemd Comfortable thro the day, had a feavour fitt at night. I was Calld at 9h morn to See mrss Kitridg who was in Labour. Shee was Deld at 10h 30m of a Daug t , her Second Child. I left her and inf t Cleverly and returnd home at 2h pm. I receivd 7/6 as a reward. at mr Kitredges. Birth 18 th . recd 7/6. Dagt Town had a feavor fitt at nig t .

G Clear and a very high wind. mr Ballard wen t to meeting. my Dag t Town is very Sick this Day, Shee Shee Sent a Bill for Prayers. mrss Densmore here foren, help t me get her up. Son and Dagt Pollard and Polly Pollard here after meeting. Hannah tarried and took Care of her Sister. at home. mrs Town is very Sick.
G Cloudy, the wind N E and very Chilly. I did not go to meeting, the rest of my famely did. mr Bradford performd. mrs s Dingley Came in to See me. Daniel Livermore here after meeting. a t home.

G X. Birth Ansel Neys 2nd Son. XX. Clear. I Did not feel very well this morn. mr Ballard, Cyrus & Watson wen t to meeting. Dagt Pollard wen Came here to See her Sister. Nabby went up to See her marm aftern. I was Calld to Ansel Neys, walkt as far as Sam l Norcros s , found the Patient in the hands of Doct Page. he gave the Case up to me and Shee was (after I removd obstructions) Safe Deld at 4h pm of a fine son. I left mother & Child Cleverly and walkt home, arivd at 8h Evn. I receivd 9/ as a reward. Doct Cony, mrs s Haywood, Straten, Toby and Dagt Lamb d & mrs Pitts here while I was absen t . Dagt Lambard Sleeps with mrs s Town y s night. at Ansel Neys. Birth 19 th . recd 9/4_as a reward. my Dagt Town is [ ] as Comfortable as Shee has been for Some Days Past.

G Clear and warm. Cyrus Came up, we all attended worship all day. Nabby at Hallowell. mr Ballard and my Self Calld at Son Lambards. Son & Dag t Pollard, mr Gill & wife, Shubal Pitts & wife there. we all partook of a good Puding & roast Veal with them. mr Lambard informs me that Dagt Town is more Comfortable and that Shee proposes to Come here Soon. may we be Seutably impres t with a Sence of Gods goodness to her. at meeting and at Son Lambards. mr Stone Discoars t from Proberbs XXII C 2 Vers, the rich and poor meet together, the Lord is the maker of them all.
G Clear and warm. Cyrus Came up. our men all attended public worship. I have felt very unwell all day but am obligd to do the work of my house. at home, unwell.

G Cloudy. mr Ballard Sett out to meeting at Augusta, was informd that mr Stone was unwell and Could not Perform. he then went to Hallowell meeting. Son & Dagt Lambard, their Childn and Son Ephm here. Jack has been not So well as Could be wisht y s day. at home. hear our Revd Pastor is unwell.
A Cloudy, misty day. what fell frose as it Came. we all tarried a t home. My husband is absen t from home. a t home.

Clear and a Cool air. we all wen t to meeting at the fort. I went to Son Jonas a t noon, find his Son very poorly. Hepsy went to ye hook at Evng. Son Jona Came here, I went home with him and took Care of his Sick Child. he was very much Distrest Some hours after I was there. got Some Sleep y e latter part of the night. at Meeting & at Son Jonas.

X. Birth James Kenny s Dagt. XX. Cloudy and Some rain. I have been very unwell but being Calld by mr James Kenny at 4h pm, I went to See his wife. Shee was Safe Deld at 5h 30 m of her Second Dag t & 4 th Child. I left her and inft very Comfortable and arivd a t home a t 10 h 30 m Evn. a t mr James Kenneys. Birth 11 th .receivd 6/ for my Service.

Clear. mr Ballard and Cyrus wen t to Town meeting. mr James was Chosen to represen t the Town of Augusta in y e Gen l Coar t this year. I have been Some more Comfortable y s day, have done part of my washing. mrs s Densmore & Livermore Calld in to See me. Watson to work at Son Jon a s. Cyrus Sleeps here. Henry m c Causland wounded his hand. at home. feel my Self a little better. mrs s Densmore & Livermore here.
Clear. we washt and washt Bed rooms & kitchen. I helpt more than I was really able as the Girl Complaind of not feeling very well. a t home.

Clear and warm. our men Planting Potatoes. Lucy Town and Rach l Capin and Allin Lambard here. Doc t Odihon Calld on us, informs that our Dagt Town is in A very low State. I wish her the aid of Divine Goodness and a resination to his will. a t home.

X. Birth W m Bells Son. XX. Clear and very warm. I was Calld at [ ]th h morn by Wil m Bell to See his wife who was in Labour. Shee was Deld at 8h 30m Evng of a Son, her first born, and is very Comfortable. mrs s M c Master and Haywood were my assistants. I tarried all night. at mr Bells. Birth 16th. receivd 12/ as a reward.

Clear and a fine air. my Dagt is very Comfortable this Day. may we take a Seutable notice of Gods goodness to her. mr Ballard & Watson been to Son Jonas to work. Revd mr Stone Came to See my Dagt & prayed with her. may God be pleasd to hear & giv an ans of peace. at home. my Dagt Seems [Cleverly] y s Day. mr Stone Came to See her, & prayed with us.

Clear. we washt. Dagt Lambard help t and I washt Some my Self. mr Ballard is gone to Sidney to Survey for mr Richardson. Old mrs s Hamlin, Burtne & Crage here aftern. Rusha Capin, Polly Hodges, Suky Haywood & Olliv, Rhoda & Hannah Pollard & Patty Livermore & Polly Smith, Sons Lamb d & Ballard been here & mr Soal. Jon a had his Shais and Carried mrs Town as far as Son Pollards. Shee was not much fatagud. a t home, we had Company.

Clear and warm. my Dear Dagt Town took her Departure from our house this morn a t 9h, homeward Bound. my God Support her on her way to her famely and prepare her mind for his will Concerning her. O Lord, the hearts of all thy Creatures are in thy hand and thou turnest them as the rivers of waters are turnd. may Shee look unto the and be Saved. Grant thy blessing to res t on her and her Famely. may they be all numbered with thy Chosen ones!!!. I have been alone this day Since My Childn left me, have felt very Gloomy. may my mind be resined to thy will in all things. at home. my Dear Dagt Town left here. mr Balld, Cyrus and watson went to Son Jona s to work. I have had a lonely day.
Clear part of the Day. I have been at home.

Birth mr Patrg s Second Dagt, help t by mrs Mosier. the inft has two thumbs on ye lef t hand. a t Ditoes. mr Partrage Came there for me. we Sett out from there, it is a tedious Snow Storm. we advanced 1 mile and broke the Sleigh. mr Patd g went back to mr Lenards where we were kindly used and refresht by food, and helpt by him & teem as far as Wilm Wards where, joind with his OXen and helpt me as far Echabod Pittses; they left us there and mr Pitts Came on with us. I wrode his hors, we find the Patient Deld of a Dag t and as well as Could be Expected. at Ditoes & mr Patrag s .

Clear. Son Jona took Breakfas t here. he is on his way to . mr Ballard wen t up to his house, Stephens wen t to get wood for him. Son Pollard Came at 30 m pm and Conducted his mother to his house. Son & Dagt Town Came here at 10h Evn and Sleep here. at home. mr Ballard is unwell, but has been ou t all day. Son Town & wife Sleep here.

Clear and Cold. I was Calld on the first hour by two Boys, who Came on foot, to go and See the wife of Sam Braly but Could not go for want of acomadation. I went to the funeral. [at the funeral of mr Tho s Pattin.]

Clear and a fine air. mr Ballard and watson workt at Son Jon a s. I washt a goun & Shirt and Cyruss Coat & waist Coat, and ironed Some. Nabby went to mr Devenports. mrs Dingly & Nancy here at Evn g . a t home.

Clear. I left mr Patridges about noon and went to mr Patens, find him very Sick. Came home at Sun Sett. Calld a t Son Lambts, their infant is Some unwell. Son Lambart Butchered our Swine this day. at mr Patridges & Pattn & Son Lamb t

X. Birth Ephm Burges 2nd Son. XX. Augs t 27 th 1799 receivd 3/7. Cloudy and Cool. I was Calld this morn to See the wife of Ephm Burges who was in Labour. Shee was Deld about 8h morn of her Second Son and 3 d Child. I lefe mother and infant Cleverly and returnd home between ten and Eleven. mr Burges paid me 6/ in part for my Service. I have Brewd a medisinal beere. mr Ballard is gone to Riedfield to Survey. Jonas hired man here helping Plough. at Ephm Burges. Birth 12th. receivd 6/. I was Cald at Evng to See genl Sewalls wife whos Breas t is Soar. I was there all night.

Clear and warm. I have workt Some in my Gardin and Cleand the Store of Cobwebs. mrs s Pattin here. mrs Dingley Calld. Nabby washt the Bed rooms and the west room. Watson wen t and rafted Bords, brot them down foren, began to Sett fens on the Entervail aflern. at home.

Clear forn, Showers aftern, with thunder. mr Ballard been to Survey for mr Briant Fletcher. a mr Hersey from Poland Sleeps here, two men by the name of Watson Dind here. at home. a mr Hersey Sleeps here.

Cloudy, raind at Evn. my Dagt Pollard and mrs s Dingley here, helpt me do my work; washt my Kitchen &C. mrs s Livermore and her Dagt Heartford Came in to See mrs Town. Shee Seems more Comfortable than Shee was yesterday. Son Town Came here aftern, his famely are well at home. a t home. my Dagt Town is a little more Comfortable. I had Company.

Clear. mr Ballard and Cyrus & Watson at work at Son Jonas. I have finisht washing, Cleand my Sellar and Done other matters about my house. Henry M c Causland Came and helpt me pull the wool of a Lamb Skin and Scoured my knives & forks. at home. mr Livermore at Son Towns y s day. my Dagt is Comfortable.
Clear. I have been at home. a t home.

Clear. I have been at home. Dagt Lambard and Child n & Becky Sewall here. our men all at work at Son Jonas. mr Lambard Came from Son Towns this morn, he informs y t my Dag t got Safe home and is as well as Shee has lately been. at home. Son & Dagt Lamb d & Childn here.

Clear and Cold. I have been at home. mr Dingley, his wife and Child n and mr Brown here to Tea. mr Ballard returnd. Betsy Hinkley Slep t here last night. Robart Fletcher Came to work here 15 inst. at home. mrs Dingley here.
Clear and very Cold. I have been at home, finisht my Self a pair hoes. mr Ballard had a Collic. at home.
Cloudy, Snowd before night. mr Dingley and Son Ephm got 2 Small Load wood. mr Ballard at mr Soals on a reference. at home.
Clear. the ice runs and y e freshet high. mr Ballard been mending the pasture fence and pu t the Cattle and Sheep in. I have been at home. at home.
Cloudy part of the day. I have done my work but was very unable. Nabby Smith Came at Evng to help me a little while. at home. Nabby Smith Came here to work.

Cloudy part of the Day. I have been at home. Son Town Sleeps here. Son and Dagt Lambard, their Son & Son Jon a Dind here. my Dagt Town remains very feeble but they think her Some better than Shee has been. a t home. Son Town Sleeps here.

X. Birth Titcomb Son. XX. Stormy, Old mr Tifany & Son Jona Came at 6h morn and Conducted me to See mrs Tetcomb. Shee was Safe Deld at 8h 30m of a fine Son, her Second Child. Dreadful Passing. at mr Titcombs. Birth 1 st . recd 12/.
Clear. mr Ballard Sett ou t to go to the meddow, Cyrus at Coar t , the young man giting wood. mr Dingley was informd that his Bror Joseph is Drownd a t Sea. at home.

Snowd, it is very moderate weather. I have been Sewing linen raggs. Hepsy bakt Bread & mins pies. mr Ballard been up Streat and down to Hollowell. Cyrus went to Cap t Gages Store and got [1] gal n molases, _ gl n W.I. Spirits on mr Burtuns acount. Son Ephm here at Evn. a t home.

Cloudy. mrss Pattin & Son & inft here. Shee wen t to the hook, informd us when Shee returnd that mr Moses Sewall departed this Life las t Evn. mr Ballard makeing a plan, Cyrus went to his Bro rs . Le faett is better. a t home.

Clear and warm. mr Ballard Sett out to get Some hay but returnd without it, the pasing being So bad. S.J. Foster here and Setled acounts with mr Ballard. I have been at home. Son Jon a here, Sup t . his son Jon a went to School and went home at nigh t . at home. S.J. Foster here, informs he has an infant 2 weaks old. we receivd Letters from Bror Jon a Moore march 5.

Clear morn, Cloudy aftern and Cool, a Clear Evng. Watson workt a t Son Jonas, he Came home before night and Conducted my Dagt and Child home. Jack tarries. I have been not so well y s day but Dagt Ballard has Done my work. at home. Dagt Balld went home. I had 7 young Ducks hach t under a hen.

G a very rainy foren, it held up at noon and mr Ballard Sett out for home, and I tarried at my Sons. Cap t Stackpool & wife, mr Fuller and his, there aftern. my Dagt was taken with fainting and Distress in her limbs. Shee and we thought her to be Expireing, but Shee revivd at Evn and gave good Exhortation to her Child n . mrs s Stackpole and I watcht with her. at my Son Towns. my Dag t had an ill turn.

G Clear and a Chilly S W wind. I felt not able to attend worship foren. I wrode behind Son Cyrus and returnd with mr Ballard. I was informd that the remains of Elijah Ingerham were interd this day. at meeting aftern. Funeral of mr Ingerhams s Son.
Cloudy. mr Ballard only, of our famely, went to meeting. a t home. Hepsy to Hinkly.
Cloudy and not very Cold. mr [Ballard and] Cyrus wen t to meeting, my Self & Hepsy tarried at home. Ezra Town and my [Son] Ephm Dind here. Son Jon a and his wife and little Son here at Evng.
Clear. my famely all attended public worship Except my Self. my Son Jonathan has seen 35 revolutions of y e sun, Just half the number of y e days of man. at home.

Cloudy. I was Calld at 4h morn by Gen l Sewall to See his Lady who was in Labour. Shee had her pains gradually till the insewing morn. mrs s Livermore went with me, we found the wife of Thom s Sewall there. I went to See mrs s Comings, find her in a low State, her Complaint is in her throat. at Genl Sewalls & Solom n Cumings.

Snowd forn, Clear aftern. I have been Sewing on two Shifts of my own, w finisht them. mr Ballard Came home, informs me that a hors had kickt Le faett Ballard on the head and hurt him very much. Col o Duttun & mr Ezkl Page here. I am informd that Capt Toby is Sick. a t home.

Clear. I Came from my Sons. Doct Cony was there to See the Child, who is not any better. I Calld at mrs Titcombs and at Son Pollards, little Sally is unwell, her mouth is Soar and her throat is Sweld some. Dagt Lambart and Child n Came home with me. my son Came for them at Evn. Cyrus at his Brothers. at Son Jonas and at my Dagts. Gen l Sewall Calld to See me this Evn. Dagt & Son Lambar t & Childn here. Snowd at Evng.

Cloudy foren, rain aftern. we had a fat Calf killd. it wd [81] lb. Dagt Lambard Came and helpt me do my work. the wido Smith Came here, they both tarry all night. at home. Dagt Lambard here, mrs Smith allso.

Clear and very warm. I tarried with my Dag t . Shee had another faint turn this aftern, but revivd. Shee Seems much resined to God s will, Even wishes for the time of her Departure. Patty Town is 15 years old this day. mrs Mary Crosby watcht. a t Son Towns, my Dagt very low.

Clear and warm. Jane Welch Came here to work. mr Ballard, P. Bullin and Jon a Brown Sett out for Poland to perform a Tour of Surveying by the apointment of the Genl Coart. they left our house at 4 h pm. I was Calld at 5 to go and See mrs s Brown. I took the keys. Bullin Came back for Papers and was oblidged to pry the hinges of y e Desk. mrs Brown Calld her women at 10h Evn. we had not much rest for the night. at mr Benn Browns, Varsalboro. the wife of Moses Ingerham Deld of a Son by Doct Cony this night. mr Balld left home for Poland.

Clear. Son Town Sleeps here. I have been very unwell but was Calld at Evn to go to See the wife of Tho s Kenny. I wrode to Esqr Smiths and went by water from there, found mrs Kenny very unwell and her women with her. at Tho s Kenneys.

Cloudy and a Sprinkling of rain. Cyrus wen t to pitts tn . mr Ballard to winthrop to See if Hepsy Brown wd Come to work for me again. I have been trying to finish my washing. mrs s Dingley Came in, made me a pres t of a pice of roas Veal. I Ead Some of it and feel refreshed. at home. mrs s Ding ly here.
Clear. Cap t Stackpole Calld here this morn, informs y t Dag t Town is very unwell. mrss Dingley & Childn here. Polly washt y e wes t room and ironed her Gown. at home. mrs s Dingley here.
Clear. I have been very unwell. I Ea t a little Cold puding and

Clear and warm. mrs s Bell was Siesd with a very Severe pain in her head a little before Day. I made use of means which gave her relief, and left her much more Comfortable at 4h pm. I was at mr Carters, Kitridges, Wessons, Pitts s , Soals and Pattins & Dingleys. at Ditoes and other Neighbours. Esqr Preskot & wife Sleep here.

Clear. my Dagt is as well as Shee was yesterday, her Phisician here, Spake Discourageing of her Case. mr Ballard & Cyrus been to Coart, Son Town Sleeps here. I had a fine nights rest. at home. mr Town Sleeps here.

Clear and Cloudy by turns. my Dag t has been much the Same as yesterday. Dagt Ballard Came, mrs Dingley, mr S. Norcros s wife & Dagt & mrss Hinkley here, Polly Livermr. my Son Came for his wife. mr Ballard returnd from Wes t Pond. Watson to work at my Sons. Nabby Smith went from here, receivd her wages. a t home. had Comp y . Nabby left me.

Some heavy Showers this aftern. I have done a larg wash and am fatagud. I brewed, allso. Dagt Lamb d Came before I had finisht, She assisted me. mr Ballard been to Jonas. a mr Sturges Calld here, informd me that Son & Dagt Town pas t by his Dadys yesterday a little Past noon. at home. Dag t Lambd & Childn here this aftern.
Clear. I have been at home. our men at Son Jonas to work. P. Barb h here. at home.
Clear part of y e day. mr Dingley and Mc Causlin here. I have been ["at home." - implied] mr Ballard Surveying for mr Thomas. at home.

Clear and pleast. Son Town & wife tarrie here, hopeing to have the road better tomorrow. mr Dingly, his wife & Childn here. mr Ballard had a fit of Collic this morn, but he went up to town aft n . I have been at home. at home. mr Ballard had y e Collic. Son & Dagt Town, mr Dingly and wife here.
Clear. mr Ballard up to town, Cyrus [ ] Jon a s [ ] home. I bakt Some apple pies & Wheat Bread. I finisht kniting Cyrus s Stockins. at home. Jon a [ went home from here. ]
Clear. mr Ballard & Watson went to Son Jonas. I have been at home. mr Ballard was informd that my Dag t Town is more unwell. may God Prepare her and all of us for his will Concerning her. at home.

raind y s morn. I went to See mrs s Cumings, find her very low. I Came home at 11 h . Old Lady Hamlin, mrs s Burtun & Doc t Odion took Tea with me. I am informd that James Andrews is wounded in his knee by the discharg of a musket. at Gen l Sewalls & mr Cuming s .
raind part of the day. I have been at home. a mr Emry from PenobScott Came here to See mr Ballard, he tarries all night. at home. A man from [Poland] here to See mr Ballard.

X. Birth Lewis Hamlins Dagt & firs t Child. X. Clear. I was Calld a t 5h 30 m to See the wife of Lewis Hamlin who was in Labour. She was Safe Deld at 4h 20 m pm of a Dagt, which w d 8 lb 2/1. I left her and infant as well as Could be Expected, at Sun Sett, and returnd home. found Polly Dingley and Polly Livermore here. mrs Dickmans Sister at George town interd this day. the wife of James Bridg was Deld this morn a t 1h of a Son, it was Born dead and is to be interd y s Evn. Doct Page was Operator, poor unfortunate man in the practice. Cap t Zimry Haywood Departed this life as he was walking from his Barn to his house, instantly by an Apoplecktic fit. may it teach us the uncertainty of Deaths aproach and inable to be allways ready. may God Sanctify the Dealings of his providence to us all, but more Especially to the Connections of the Decesed. at Lewis Hamlins. Birth 15 th . recd 12/ July 5th '99. Birth James Bridges Son, it was Born Dead. their first Born and intered this Evng. Capt Zimry Haywood Expired this Evng.

Clear foren, Showers aftern. mr Bullin and wife Dind and took Tea with us. I am Destitute of help. Dag t Lambd Came at Evn, tarries all night. Dagt Town has had a Comfortable day and rested tolerable well. at home, had Comp.

Clear and very warm. our men a t Jonas to work. mr Jos p Whites wife here. mr Page Calld as Shee was going to Lecture. I have been doing my house work and Some in My Gardin. a t home, mrs s White here.
Clear and hott. mrs s Dingley Came and brot the Goun Shee had made. I washt that and Some Other things. Dagt Lamb d went home this morn. at home.

Clear. I have been at home till Sun Sett , was then Calld to See the wife of Isaac Stone who is Extreamly ill with the Collic. I tarried and watch t . the red pepper was what Seemd to reliev her. mr Town Conducted Dagt Pollard home, Says his wife is Comfortable. at mr Isaac Stones.
Cloudy morn, raind before noon. I have knit Some, gatherd Cucum s , Camomile, merigolds and pickt Some wool. Jane Did hous work and Spun yarn for a Mop. at home.
Clear and very Cold. mr Ballard is better. I was Calld to go to Isaac Cowens at 9h Evn, arivd there at 11. at Isaac Cowens, Sidny.
Clear part of the day. mr Ballard been to Son Jonas. I have been at home. at home.
raind. I have been at home. mr Ballard to Col o Howards mill, out to mr Bullins and to Hallowell. a t home. workt a little in my gardin.

Clear. Son Town left here. mr Ballard had a turn of the Collic. I was Calld to mr Bells to See their infant who has the thrush, his wife is recovering. I was at mr Kitredges and Hamlins & Patredges & at mrs Patins. a young woman of Coulor Expired there this aftern. at mr Bells and other famelys. Polly Cooper Expired this aftern.

Clear. I have been helping do the house work. Dagt Ballard Came here, Shee thinks of going to Winslow tomorrow. I went to See Nancy Dingley who is unwell, her Dady & marm are gone to winslow. Jane is not very well this Evng. I gave her Cammomile Tea and Shee got Cleverly by morn. at mr Dingleys. Nancy is unwell. A barn was Consumed by fire a t Reedsfield last thursday, it belonged to A mr Smith.
G Clear part of the day, misty by turns. mr Ballard and Cyrus attended worship at Augusta, Hepsy at Hallowell. I have been at home. Dagt Lambard wrode here after meeting. at home. Dagt Lambard here.

G Clear. our famely all attended worship foren. I went aftern. Polly Coopers remains were interd in the Burying Ground of Esqr Cony after the Evng Service. Nabby Smith attended. Son Cyrus went to Pitts tn after meeting. at meeting & at Son Lambards. the Text in John 14 th C, 1st vers.
rainy. I was at home, helpt wash. at home.
[PAGE REPAIR COVERS THIS ENTRY.]
Clear and Cold. Cyrus went to Pittst n . I have been mending Legins and Glovs and &c. Hepsy washt. at home.

X. Birth Genl Henry Sewalls 4th Daug t . XX. Clear. mrss Sewall was Safe Deld at 3h y s morn of a fine Daugt, her 7 th Child. I left her Cleverly and returnd home a t 9 h 30m. I receivd 6/7 as a reward. mrss Livermore tarries with her till he went to magr FileBrowns for her nurs. mr Ballard been to Son Jonas, all well there. I have had a very bad head aick this aftern & Evn. at Genl Sewalls. Birth 8th. receivd 6/7 as a reward.
Clear part of the day. I help t Hepsy wash and knit some. Allin Lambard with me while his dad went to p tstn . mr Ballard workt at Son Jonas, Jack Came home with him. at home.

G Clear. we all attended meeting. the Ordinance of the Supper was Administred. I went to mr Dingleys, Nancy is better. they informd me that Dagt Town has been more Comfortable y s Two Days, for which fav r I would Bless the Great Author of all our mercies. Son Ephm and Jery Heartford took Tea with us. I was Calld about Sun sett to See the wife of Zyba Petingail who was in Labour, bu t not very ill. I tarried all night. had no Sleep. at meeting. the Lords Sp r administrd. I was at mr Dingleys & informd y t Dagt Town is revivd a little. was at mr Petingals. Son Ephm here. this is the aneversarey of the Birth of Dagt Lambard, 26 years Since.
Clear and warm. I have been at home, harvisted part of my beens, Jane washt. at home.

Clear and pleasant. mr Ballard and I went to Son Jonas, his Son Le faett is more Comfortable than I Expected to find him. he Complains mostly of his Swiling under his arm. we Calld at Son Lambarts, all well. I began a Stockin for mr Ballard. at my Son Jonas & Lambts.
a very rainy day. Dag t Lamb d went home aftern. mrs Smith tarries. I have been mending Stockins, feel but feable. at home.

G Clear part of the day. I attended public worship at the fort. Nabby Andrews a t meeting. old mr Pollard Calld to See us and took Tea here. mr Pollard informd me of a woman up where he livs who has lately been Deld of two Child n , and there were 8 weaks between the Births. at Meeting. mr Bond had prayr s on the Death of his mother. remarkable Birth of Twins.

Clear. I Sett out from Son Towns for home, left my Dagt Some revived. may the Great God Support her thro all he Sees fitt to lay upon her in y s tife and Croun her with glory here after. may he be a very present help to her ConSort and Childn and all Connections!!! I Calld at mrs Capins and Rufus Ballards and mr Ben n Browns on my ret n . at Son Towns. Cyrus went to Son Towns.

X. Birth Benn Browns Dag t & 11 Child. XX. Clear Except a Shower a t Evn. mrs s Brown was Deld of a Dagt, her 11th Child, at 11h 30 m . I left her and inft as well as Could be Expected and reacht home before Sun Sett. mr Brown Gave me 6/ Cash and a Chees, w d 7 lb, for my Service. his Son Natl Came for the hors, he Sleeps here this night. at mr Browns. Birth 23 d . I heard, a t mr Thoms, that Dag t Town is no better.
Clear. I have been Doing hous work and taking Care of my sage. Watson went to work for Son Jon a . Eunice Did my washing. at home.
Clear. I was with my Patient but Shee is more Comfortable. the women went home Excep t her Sis t Blanchard. a t Ditoes.
Clear. I have been at home. mr Ballard & Watson went to meeting, it is a Continentall Fas t . at home.
Clear. I have been at home doing about the hous. mr Martin, who lives with Son Jona, been here to work this and yesterday. Watson went there to work, they took my Loom to Son Pollards. at home.

Cold milk twice in the Coars of y e day and performd part of my washing. I Laid my Self on the Bed in the bed room, was not able to rise from there. my Husband wen t to bed and not Come to See me, So, I lay there in my Cloaths till 5 hours morn when I made Shift to rise. I got the men Breakfas t , but was not able to Eat a morsil my Self till after 3pm, but I finisht my washing. how many times I have been nesesatated to res t my Self on the bed, I am not able to Say. God grant me patience to go thro the fatagus of this life with fortitude, looking forward for a more happy [state]. at home. mrss Livermore Calld here. Shee is gone to Hallowell.

Clear and hott fore noon. I went to mr Bells, find her more Comfortable. I was at Lewis Hamlins, his wife is Cleverly; at mr Crags, Pegy very low. I Calld at mr Heartfords, his wife Deld of two Dead Babes by Esqr Cony this morn, one of Each Sex. at mr Bells and others. Birth. mrs Heartford twins, a Son & Dagt.

Clear. Raind this Day. I was Calld to See the wife of Ansel Ney, Shee was very unwell all day. I tarried with her all night. Shee had Some rest after midnight. mrss Ney and Greely there. at Ans l Neys. Son Town Sleeps here.

Birth Printer Robinsons Son & Second Child. Clear. I was Calld at 4h y s morn to See mrss Robinson, wife to the printer. Shee was Deld of a fine Son before I arivd. Shee was alone at the Birth, her Neighbour women had got her to bed. I Drest the infant, took Breakfast and returnd. mr Ballard and Cyrus went to Jonas. the wives of James & Thos Hinkley Spent the aftern here. we are informd that Son & Dagt Town went Pas t Levy Robinsons this morn. at mr Robinson s . Birth 21st. two mrsss Hinkleys here.

Clear Except Some Showers. mr Ballard and Watson at Jonas foren, they finisht giting the Hay there. Cyrus began to pull our flax. I have Bakt and done my work and done Some in the Gardin. Watson was informd that my Dagt Town was very Sick yesterday. at home, had [heavy] News from Dagt Town.
Cloudy and Some rain. I have been mending Shirts and kniting and doing part of the house work. at home.

X. Birth Richd Fosters 2nd Son and 3 d Child. XX. Clear. mrss Foster was Safe Deld at the 12h of a fine Son her 3 d Child, which w d 14 lb. I left her Cleverly at 3 and arivd at home at 7 h Evng. I find that I have had 2 Calls Sinc I have ben gone, one by mr Robbins and one by mr Cleark. God Grant them Safe Deliverance. at mr Fosters. Birth 25 th . receivd 6/ Oct 21 st of mr Fost r at Son Lambards.

Clear. I have been doin house work, Churnd and pu t a kettle of Greas & Ley to boil. Eunice been Spining and helpt me Some. Watson workt with thee OXen for mr Craig. I wrote to my Husband. at home, wrote to my Husband By mr David Densmore.

Clear and very warm. I have been to Hallowell at Divers Stores, was at Moses Parmers, receivd 9/ of his wife for assisting her in Child birth. I went to See mrs s Bell aftern, find her and infant more Comfortable, mrs Loes Harris Nursing her. I Calld at Sons Pollards & Lambards. mr Pollard has been to his Fathers, they are well Except his Dady. he was at Son Towns, my Dagt is better. a t Hallowell and at mr Bells. at Son Pollards & Lamb ts .
Clear. I have been at home doing my hous work. Son Jona Dind here. at home.
Some rain. mr Ballard returnd at noone. mr Emry & mr Georg Brown Sleep here. I have been at home. at home.
Clear. I have been at home. mr Ballard finisht his Business on a road from Augusta to Sidney and returnd home. a t home.

Birth Abel Chases Son and first Born. Clear Excep t Showers. I was Calld at 1h 30 m to go and See the wife of Abel Chase who was in Labour. Shee was Deld at 5 h 30m of A fine Son, her first Born. I left her and inft as well as Could be Expected and returnd home at 9 h fore n . my Dagt Town had a Severe fitt of Ague and feaver. mrs Dingley Came in and help t me. mrss Livermore & Densmore here aftern. my Dagt had a faint turn at Evn. I gave her Caster and Shee revivd. a t Abel Chases. returnd at 9h m. myDag t Town had a Shakeing fit, began at 10, and a Severefeaver fit after it. Birth 20 th .

Clear and warm. our men to work at Jonas. Dagts Pollard & Lambard with their Childn here. we went to See mrs s Dingley aftern. Dolly, with her Childn, Came home with me and Sleep here. at mr Dingleys & Livermores. my Dagts here.

Clear and very warm. I have been very unwell. mrs Toby & Strat n here, they washt my Dishes and made my beds. Dagt Pollard Came and Cookt me Some Dinner and Did other matters. Capt James Springer took Tea here. at home. Dagt Pollard here, infmd me y t that Mos s Carter had a Dag t Born on wednsdy.

raind. I was at Son Towns. my Dagt is very weak and Low, her mouth and throat are very Soar and her Bones are Deprived of flesh to Cover them. I pray God to grant her his imediate presence & Support. at Son Towns, my Dagt very low.
Cloudy, raind at night. I have been at home, Did part of my house work. at home.

Clear. I left mrs Stone Easy and Came to my Dagt Pollards, took Breakfas t with her and Came home, wrode Son Lambar d s hors. our men all at Jonas. Son & Dagt Lamb t and Ephm & mrs Heartford been here. at Dito & Son Pollards.

Clear. I gatherd musturd and did other matters in my Gardin, was Calld at 6h Pm to go and See the wife of Richd Foster of winthrop, find her Some more Comfortable than when She Sent for me. I tarried all night, mrs French allso. at Richd Fosters.
Clear. I have been at home, Sorted my Apples and Cleand my Chambers. Cyrus went to his Brothers. Watson went to his Fathers. at home.
A very rainy day and night. I have been at home doing my hous work and mending. at home. watson put a partition in the Sellar.
G Cloudy all day, raind Some. Cyrus Came up and returnd to Pitts tn again. Ephm Dind here. we all tarried at home. mr Stone is absent to an Ordination. at home. Cyrus and Ephm here.

G Clear. the Girls and Cyrus went to meeting. I tarried at home, had the rooms all to Sweap and a Diner to Cook. Son and Dagt Pollard & Rhoda and Sis t Williams, Son & Dagt Lamb d , Ephm & Allin dind with me. at home. Sister Williams Sleeps here. Dagt Pollo Says her Sist Town is very Sick.
G rainy. I was at mr Fosters, his wife not very Sick. mrs French there. a t Ditoes.
G Clear Excep t a Shower. I tarrid at home, our men went to Meeting. mr Martin and Son Ephm [ ] me and dind with us after meeting.

Cloudy and Some rain. Watson workt for Son Jon a . mr Ballard Setled with Saml & Phillip Norcross and took their obligations for what was Due to him. he went up to mill afterwards, Sett out to run the South line of the plymoth Companies tand. he left home at 1h pm. Nabby washt. I have washt the Store floor. mr Ballard Saw Son Town who informs that his wifeis Some better.
Clear and hot. I washt my Cloaths in one water. mrs Pikf d , P. Dingly and Nabby Livermore & 3 Densmor Girls here. at home. our men at Jonas.
Clear. I was at mr Fosters, his wife not very well. at Ditoes.

Clear. I was at mr Petingals. my Patient Lingars. mrs s Fletcher Calld there y s morn. Shee and I went to mr Allins. the women Calld to See mrs Petingl a t midnight. at mr Petingls & Allins. mr Hill and RachelSavage were Married.

Cloudy. Son Ephm and Pevis Hamlin & Sister Wilms took Breakfast with me. Sister went to pitts tn by water with them, Cyrus went by land to mr Moors to tend mill. I have Cleand my Seller and Pickt Beens of the vines. Jane Did the washing, Eunice Spun. Watson returnd a t Evng. James Moore here, informs me his youngest Child is Dead. at home. Sister Willm s wen t to pits tn , Cyrus allso.
rainy. I have been at home, done the house work and mended a Coat for my Self and knitt Some on my Stockin. at home.

Clear and very Pleasant. I have been piking wool and Doing Some matters in my Gardin. I finisht kniting a pair Cottne & Tow hoes for my Self y e 16 th inst. at home. Dagt Lamb d here, had our hors to Sidney.
Cloudy morn, the Sun shone a t the 11 th hour. mr Ballard is gone to Son Jonas, Watson to work w h our OXen for Shubl Pitts. I have been at home. At home. Death G. Boltons wife.
rainy. I was at home, Cut apples for pies and Sause. mr Ballard up Streat. at home.

Birth Seips Dagt.] Clear. I was Calld to See Lidia Sips wife at 3h pm. Shee was Deld at 8 Evn of a Dagt. I tarried all night, mrs Cain & Ben Whites wife allso. we had no where to Sleep so we Sett up. at Seips. Birth 7th. all Cleverly.

Cloudy and Clear by turns. I have been at home, finisht kniting Cyrus a pair of hoes and began a pair for mr Ballard. he has been to Son Jonas. Hannah Mags here, Shee informd me that a man of Colour was Drowned at Hallowell yesterday. at home.

Clear and warm. there has been a review on the parade by mr Hinkleys. my Dagts and Sister Williams Came here and went down to See the maneuvers. my Dagts went home and Sister Sleeps here. I wrote to Doct Barton and to Bro r Collins Moore. at home. wrote Brothers Collins and Barton.
Cloudy morn, raind after noone and night. I went to Son Lambards and Pollards, I find them all about, heard that my Dagt Town was very ill first day night. at my Sons Poll d & Lamb ts .

rainy foren. Dagt Lamb d and mrss Dingly have been makeing a Goun for my dr Dag t Towns las t dress. mr Ballard has been unwell with a Collic. Cyrus returnd with news that his Sister is much revivd. at home, hear by Cyrus that my Dagt is more Comfortable.
Clear. I have been mending a Skirt and bed quillt. I wen t to See mrs s Dingley aftern. mrs Page Calld here. at mr Dingleys.

Clear and very Pleast. Watson workt on the road with the OXen. Eunice washt. I Did the house work, Cleand the Store Chamber and knitt Some. A mr Smith of Faett Steeps here. am informd that Doct Amasa Dingley is dead. at home, was infmd of the Death of Doct Dingly.
Cloudy and Some rain. I tarried with my Patient this Day. Shee washt and Bak t . a t Ditoes.

Clear. mr Ballard & Son Pollard went to take their Sheep ou t of pound a t Hallowell. Old mr Lambard Calld to See me, his famely are well. Watsons Brother dind here. Polly Livermore Came in and helpt me. Polly Barbarick Came to work here. at home. Son Town Dind here. mrs Dingley Calld [here].

Clear. I was Calld by Saml Cony at 3h morn to go and assist to put Grave Cloaths on the remains of Sol mn Cumings wife who Expired y s ["morn" - implied]. mrs Mc Master & Fletcher assisted me. at mr Cumings s , his wife Expired a t 3 hours morn.

Clear morn. I left my patient better and returnd home, find my Dagt as well as I left her. mrs Wing & Thomas and Polly Smith here. Nabby went to See if Nelly Googin would Come and help me but did not Suckseed. a t Ditoes. Son Town here y s night.

Clear and very warm. Esq r Preskott and two other men Dind here. mrs s Dingley Came to See a Barril Pork divided. Shee helpt me about giting Diner. I had a Sharp turn of Collic, got Ease Soon by takeing a Decoction of the read Peppers. mr Ballard went from home with Esqr Preskott to run a line. at home.
Clear. I have been at home. Son and Dagt Lambard here, they Sleep here with their Child n . at home.
Clear. I have been at home. Cyrus went and brot 2 Bushl of Apples from the farm above. I have pickt Some wool and Done Some thing in the Gardn. at home.

Clear morn, Cloudy at 10h m . mr Ballard & Watson working on the road forn. we attended Lecture af tn . I went to mr Dingleys after I came home, was at mr Livermores. mrs Peckford brot there, very unwell. at Leclure & at mr Blacks & Dinglys and Livrmrs s .
Clear morn. mr Brown & Emry left here. mr Ballard went to Son Jon a s. Polly Smith Dind here. a t home.

Clear. my Dag t is more Comfortable than Shee was yesterday. Shee rode as far as the fense by y e road twice this day. Mrs s Dingley here foren. Dagt Pollard, mr s Pattin & mr James Hinkleys wife took Tea with us. at home.

Clear and warm. Dagt Lamb d tarried all day with her babe. mr Ballard Carried Allin home as he went to Jonas. Cyrus went there. Phillip Bullin here y s aftern. at home. Dagt Lamb d help t me do my work.

Clear and warm. I have felt not So well as I Could wish. Patty Livermore and Polly Hodges Came and helpt me do my work. mrs s Livermore Came in to See me. Jane Welch here, promest to Come and work for me next weak. I was Calld by mr Floid a t 10h Evn to go to See the wife of a mr Wood, of winthrop, who was in Labour. I arivd about mid night, find the Patient very ill. I Spent the night without any Sleep. at mr Woods of winthrop.

Clear. my Son Jon a & I left Son Towns and reacht home before Sun Sett. it has been very Cold y s day. I left my Dear Dagt a little more Comfortable than Shee was when I went there. Watson got in the last of our flax y s Evng. Jane returnd here this Morn. at Son Towns. my Dagt Seems a little revivd. mrss Hynes watcht with her last night.
Clear. I have been at home Puting my house in order &C. at home.
G Clear. I attended worship aftern. Calld at Son Lambards, he and my Dagt Came here after meeting. Cyrus at home. mr Lamb d informs y t my Dagt Town is Some more Comfortable. at meeting.

G X. Birth Suky Kenadys Son. XX. Cloudy, Snowd Some and was very Cold. mr Ballard & Cyrus went to meeting. I was Calld at 9h 30 m Evng to mr Kenadas to See his Dagt Suky, who was in Labour. her Child was Born a few muinets before I arivd. I Performd all that was necessary and we put her to Bed Comfortable. I tarried all night. they Say that Wilm Ellis is the father of y e Child. at mr Kenadys. Birth 29 th . May 14 th 99 recd my fee in Spining.

G XX. Birth mr Woods Son & firs t Born. XX. Clear and very warm. mrss Wood was Safe Deld at 6h morn of a fine Son. I left her and inft Comfortable, and reacht home at 10. I went onto the Bed and Slept till aftern. Son and Dag t Lambard and Childn, Becky Sewall, mrs s Dingley and mrs Eunice Stackpole here after meeting. I receivd 9/ of mrss Wood Sept 12, 1798 for my Service there this day. at mr Woods. Birth 22 nt . mrs Dingley informs me that Dagt Town remains much as Shee was.

G Clear. mr Ballard and the young men went to meeting. my Dagt Town rode on a hors to the road this morn & in a Shais aftern, as far as mr Hinkleys; her husband with her. Doct Odion here to See her. mr Gill & wife, wido Savage, Son Jona and wife, my Dagt tarries, Son & Dagt Lambt. at home. Son Town Came. my Dagt has been not So ill as on friday. Hannah More [Calld].

G Clear and pleas t . I have been at home very unwell. Cyrus Came up home. he, Walson and Jane went to meeting. Augustus Ballard Came, dind, went aftern. his Mother Came and tarried all night. Son & Dagt Lamb d & Son & mrss Dingley took Tea. at home. feel very unwell but had to work all day. had Some Company. Jane went to Son Lamb ds .

Clear. I went to See mrs Pegy Craig, find her very poorly. Calld to See mr Thwing, find him very much distress t with the tisick. I was a t Lues Hamlins, they are well; at mr Carters, all well there. Rufus Ballard Sleeps here. I was informd that Bro r Jon a s famely were well not long Since. at mr Craggs & others. Nabby at mrs Dingly s . I heard from Rochester. mr Bro r & famely well.

Clear and very pleast. Watson went to work for Son Jona. Sister Wil ms went home, wrode our Mare and is going on to See my Dagt Town and tarrie with her a little time. at home. Sister Williams went from here.
Cold and Squally. I was at home. mrs Pierce went from here attended by mr Kimbal in a Shais. mr Ballard Surveying for Doc t Cony. at home.

Snowd. mr Ballard went to the hook, bot _ G n Spirt, 2 lb Sugar, 2 do Candles. I Came home from mr Kenadys, left the Patients Cleverly. I have been all most Sick all day. Sally has done my work and mended. at Ditoes.
Clear and Pleast. I have been at home. we had a Swine Butchered, w d 202. at home.

Clear Excep t Some Showers. I have washt my Cloaths in the 2nd water. our men at my Sons. mrs Dingly & Child n here. mr Livermore Saw my Dagt Town yest d . Shee was as well as [ ]. I Sent a bottle of Current Syrryp, a Sheet Shift & hand kerChief to my Dear Dagt Town by mr Straten. at home. at home. mrs Dingly & Child n here.

Clear. mrs Foster Seemd to be more Comfortable. I left her afler breakfast and arivd at home at 30m Pm. mr Foster attended me. I went to Son Lambards, he came from Son Towns at 11h morn. my Dagt is very low. I pray for Divine Support for her and famely. at Ditoes and Son Lambard s .

X. Birth Zeba Petingls Dagt & firs t Born. Cloudy morn. mrss Petingail was Deliverd of A fine Dag t at 1h 40m this morn. I left her and infant Cleverly and arivd at home at 4h and 20m, went to bed and Slept till 8. have been Since Doing matters about house. at mr Petingls. Birth 24th. I receivd 12/ as a reward for my Service.
A Severe Storm of rain & wind. I have been at home. at home.
Clear and pleast. I have done my house work and knitt Some, am informd that Dagt Town is better. God Grant it may be so. at home.
Clear and very Pleast. I have been picking wool and doing other matters about house. at home.

Clear. mr Ballard went to the fort and hook. Cap t Hammond and his wife Dind here. I have been doing house work and knitt, was informd that Georg Boltons wife Expired yesterday and her remains were interd this aft n . Eunice washt, I did the hous work. at home. mr Ham n [&] wife here. Burial mrs s Bolton.
Clear morn. I have been Bakeing Pies. mr Ballard up Streat, Cyrus after Vinegar and other matters. Phillip Bullin Sup t with us. a t home. Phillip Bullin Sleeps here.

Clear. Mr Ballard and Cyrus to Town meeting. mr Brown Came for his Dag t Hepsy and Shee Set out with him a t 1 h pm. mrss Brown is very Sick, her life no t Expected. Jack Ballard here, he helpt me Some, [ ] I did the hous work. at home. Hepsy went home to See her Sick mother.

Cloudy morn, raind aftern. Son Lambards Dagt had fitts this morn, he Came for me. I went and found the babe a little revivd. mrs s Haywood there. I administred a Clister which gave great relief. I tarried all night. Son Lamb d & Pollard and his wife went to Son Towns. Sister Williams went from here to Son Pollards and tarries there while they are gone. at Son Lambard s , their Babe had a fittthis morn.
Cloudy foren, Cleard of aftern, a very Pleas t Evn g . mr Densmore & wife, mrs Thomas, Mary and the Widdow Forbes took Tea with me. Esq r Wesson Calld here. at home, had Comp y .

G Clear morn, Some Snow fel in the past night. mr Ballard and I attended worship all day. the Ordinance of the Supper was Administred. mrss Lamb partook with us. I was Calld at Sun Sett to go to Esqr Haywoods and See his Sick Children, the Dagt was very ill all night. at meeting and at Esqr Haywoods, watcht with his Childn.
G A very rainy Day. our men went to meeting. I tarried at home. at home.

Clear and Some Cooler. mrs Dingley Calld here going to y e hook. Shee Bot me Some pins and needles. I went to Son Lamb ds & Pollards, hear that Dagt Town is much as Shee was. I went to Hamlins and Carters, bot 1 pr Cards, 1 DZ n Biskit, Sheet g Board, Cost 5 s ;2 d . at Sons Polle s & Lamb s ,receivd a Letter from Bro r Jon a , his famelyare well.
Clear. Watson finisht geting our potatoes in. I have Done my house work and knitt Some. at home.

Birth mr Thwings Dag t . Cloudy. I have Brewd & Bakt, and was Calld after Sun Sett to mr Thwings to See his Lady who was in Labour, and was Deld of a Dagt at 8h Evn. I walkt to mr Thwings. he Saw mr Ballard returning from Son Towns, who informd him that our Dear Dag t Departed this life this Day. I returnd home about 10h Evn, when mr Ballard infmd me Shee Expired at 10h this morn. may God grand us all hearts to Submit to his will and to hear the Call, be allso ready. at mr Thwings. Birth 27 th . recd 6/as rew d . Death of my Dag t Town, aged 40years, 2 months and 11 days.

Clear. I returnd home a t 10h morn, find my hous alone and Every thing in arms, did not find time to Set down till 2 pm. mr Shubal Hinkleys wife here to See me. Elisha Noles here, paid me in Cloath for assisting his wife. a t Ditoes this morn. mrs Hinkley here afternoon.
Clear. mr Ballard and Cyrus went to Coart. I have been at home. mrs Town is not So well y s day. mrss Densmore & Dingley here foren. at home. Son Town Sleeps here.

Clear till the aftern when we had a very heavy Shower with heavy thunder and Sharp lightning. my Dagt Lambar d was here. mrs Pitts Calld here. mr Ballard returnd before it raind much. mr Martin here to work. I have had two informations this Day; that my Dear Dagt Town is thought to be very near the Close of life. may God prepare her and all of us for his will Concerning her. a t home.

Clear. I was Calld before Day to See the wife of Jessy Cleark, tarried all day. I went to See Uriah Clearks wife who is very unwell. my Patient Seems to be more Comfortable. at Jessy Clearks and at his dads.

Clear. I have been overhauling my wool and Sorting it. mrss Dingly Sent for me aftern, Shee is unwell. mrs Densmore Came there. I Sent for Jane to tarry with her and Came home my self. at mr Dingleys, his wife is unwell.

Clear. mr Ballard Surveying for mr Vauhn. I went to See Pegy Crage, find her very Low. I was at Son Lambards. mr Ben n Browns wife there. Lidia Densmore here. Watson Sett ou t to go to his Fathers at Evng. at mr Craggs & Lambard s .

Clear and warm. our men went to work at Son Jonas. Meriah Grifin Came and washt for me. I Did my house work and washt the Bed rooms & Kitchen. this day is the Anniversary of my Dagh t Pollards Birth. at home. Meriah Grifin washt for me. I paid her 2/ for her Service. we are informd Dagt Town was very Low las t tuesday.

Clear part of the day. I Came home Early y s morn. mr Ballard and Cyrus went to Town meeting. Otis Pierce Conducted his Sister, Hitty, to See me. Shee tarries all night, her Babe is with her. at Esqr Haywd s . mrs Hitty Pierce here on a visit.

Cloudy, Snowd at Evn. mr Ballard wen t to winthrop, Carried 25 yds of white woolen Cloath to be Coulered and pres t . I was Calld to See mrss Dingley again, find her very ill. I tarrid all night, Sett with her. Shee Seemd Easier before morn, but her throat is very much Sweld inwardly. at mr Dingleys, Shee is very Sick. mr Heartford & wife allso

Clear. mr Ballard Sett out to Lay out a County road from Moses Pollards to Winslow. I have been at home, washt Some. mrs s Livermore Came in here, her Dagt Patty Sleeps here. at home. mr Ballard gone to Survey a road.
Snowd and is Cold & windy. I have been at home.
Clear and Pleasant. I was Calld a t 10h Evn to See the wife of mr Philip Norcross who was in Labour. mrss Randal & Hankerson there. at mr Phillip Norcross.

Clear, Snowd at Evn. Dagt Lamb d and Child n and Becky Sewall Came here this morn, they made me a winter Goun. Sons Lambard and Ephm Came at Evn and Conducted them home. I was Calld a t 11 h 30 m to See the wife of Shubael Pitts who was in Labour. at home till after 11 O Clock at night. Dagt Lambd made me a goun, Becky here with her.

Clear. I have helpt Do my hous work and wen t to the hook. bot 1 y d of muslin for Dagt Lambard and 1 y d for my Self at J.O. Pages @ 7/6 pr y d . Cap t Nichols from Oxford and mr Moore from Pitts tn here. at the hook.
Clear. I have been at home, Did house work. Son Jona, mrs Cypher & Welch Dind here. Jane went home with them. at home.
Clear and very Pleas t . I have been at home, pikt wool. at home. finisht my
Clear. I have been doing my house work. mr Ballard been to Son Jonas, Eunice Spun and Watson Bankt the house. at home. Cap t Ab m Pages oldest Son Expird.

X. Birth Asa Williams Dag t . XX. Cloudy and Some Snow. mrs Williams was Safe Deld at 3h pm of her 6th Dagt and 7 th Child. I left her Cleverly and Came to Son Jonas where I tarried all night. he has been to our house with all his Child n , left Jack and brot the others home. this is y e Aniversary of my mariage, 44 yrs. at mr Asa Williams s . Birth 35th. ISlept at my Sons.

Clear. I have been at Son Lamb ds part of the day. we gave the babe Rheubarb and C m tart r , it Seems much better. I went to Son Pollards and Spoold 14 Skeins hoes yarn and Sett out for home. Spake with mrs s Densmore who informd me my husband was well last Lords Day. at Son s Lamb d & Pollard s . little Dolly is better. hear that mr Ballard was well.

Clear and Pleasant. Jane Welch left me. I paid her for her Labour when Shee gets her Shoes f m Child s . Eunice went to Doct Colmans and promised to get here on Thurdsday next. I went to mr Dingleys at Evn. Joseph White Expired. at mr Dingley s . Death of Joseph White.

Clear and pleast. I Saw Doc t Odion, he informs me my Dag t Town was as well as Shee has been somtime pas t . I have Bakt and done the most of the house work, Cutt Apples to Dry. Watson workt for Esqr Haywood with our OXen. a t home.

Clear and pleast. I Sett out for winslow at noon, went as far as mr Capins where Son Jona & Lambard & wife overtook me. Jona and I went on to Son Towns, they tarried at mr Capins. we arivd a t 8h 30 m Evng. mrss Strattne and Patty Collar were there, but went home after we arivd. at Son Towns, Son Jona allso.
Clear and very Pleast. I have been at my Sons. mrss Robinson of Sydny and mrss Saunders there. at my Sons.

Clear morn, Showers aftern. mr Ballard & Cyrus went to Coart, it ajourned this aftern. Son Town went home. Dagt Lambard here, mrs s Livermore allso. Dagt Town has been very feeble all day. a t home. Cousin Hains Learned Dined here.
Clear and hott. mr Ballard and I went to Son Towns, we found our Dagt more Comfortable than Shee has been for days Past, but very Low. at Son Towns.

raind this morn, Cloudy part of the day. I left mrs Cleark about house and returnd as far as Son Lambards. mrss Capins there. we went in at Son Pollards.
